539MP/NỀN TẢNG TỪ VỰNG - ĐỌC HIỂU/20 CHỦ ĐỀ TỪ VỰNG VÀ ĐỌC ĐIỀN - ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN/15 Chủ đề 4 Social Issues (2).pdfok315 / 0 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:15missing:15
Passage passage-c34bfcbbea-01-a6c48b49b1 (3 questions)
Living in the city offers many advantages. First of all, people have better access to education, healthcare, and job opportunities. Big cities usually have modern infrastructure, with convenient public transport and various entertainment options like cinemas, shopping malls, and restaurants. Life in the city is fast-paced and exciting, especially for young people who want to explore new things and grow their careers. However, city life also comes with several disadvantages. The cost of living is often high, and housing can be expensive and crowded. Many people suffer from noise pollution and air pollution, which negatively affect their health. Traffic jams and overpopulation make daily life stressful. In addition, some people may feel lonely or disconnected because of the busy and competitive environment. Although city life can be full of opportunities, it is not always easy or peaceful.
Q1 According to paragraph 1, in cities, people have better access to all of the following, EXCEPT _______.
A. education | B. healthcare | C. housing | D. job opportunities
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q2 The word “crowded” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. empty | B. populous | C. bustling | D. huge
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q3 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. City residents enjoy peaceful lives with little noise or traffic problems. | B. Urban living provides access to jobs, services, and modern entertainment options. | C. People in cities rarely feel lonely because they are always surrounded by others. | D. Housing in big cities is cheap and available for most working-class families.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-c34bfcbbea-02-411754effe (4 questions)
Cyberbullying is one of the most serious problems caused by social media. Unlike face-to-face bullying, it can happen anytime and reach a wide audience within seconds. Victims may receive hurtful messages, be mocked through memes, or have private information shared online without consent. This often leads to stress, anxiety, and even depression. Peer pressure is another harmful effect. On social media, teens often compare themselves to others and feel the need to follow trends just to fit in. They may post certain photos, wear specific brands, or act in ways they normally wouldn’t, just to gain likes or avoid feeling left out. This pressure can damage their confidence and lead to unhealthy behaviours. Fake news spreads quickly on social platforms, especially when users don’t verify the information before sharing. False stories about health, politics, or celebrities can mislead the public and cause real- world consequences. In some cases, fake news even sparks fear or hatred within communities, making social media a dangerous place for truth.
Q4 The word “private” in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. safe | B. secret | C. important | D. public
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q5 The word “They” in paragraph 2 refers to _______.
A. Teens | B. Others | C. Trends | D. Certain photos
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q6 The word “consequences” in paragraph 3 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______.
A. abilities | B. opportunities | C. outcomes | D. advantages
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q7 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Cyberbullying usually happens face-to-face and causes little emotional harm. | B. Peer pressure on social media builds confidence and encourages healthy habits. | C. Fake news spreads slowly because users often check facts before sharing. | D. Social media can harm teens through bullying, pressure, and false information.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-c34bfcbbea-03-8b3785b09a (8 questions)
Gender inequality has existed for centuries in many parts of the world. It refers to the unfair differences in treatment and opportunities between men and women. While many people believe that modern society has overcome this issue, the truth is that gender inequality still appears in both subtle and obvious ways. In daily life, it is not hard to notice how women are sometimes expected to stay at home, take care of the family, or accept lower-paying jobs. In some areas, girls are still denied access to proper education, while boys are encouraged to pursue their dreams. Even in developed countries, it is common to see fewer women in leadership roles or high-paying positions. The impact of this issue is far-reaching. When half of the population is not given equal chances, society loses out on countless talents and ideas. Gender inequality can also harm mental health, limit personal growth, and create an unfair environment where some voices are always louder than others. Solving this problem requires more than just laws. It takes education, open conversations, and a change in mindset. Everyone plays a part in building a world where gender no longer decides a person’s worth.
Q8 The word “obvious” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to_______.
A. fixed | B. complex | C. easy | D. clear
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q9 The word “their” in paragraph 2 refers to ______.
A. areas | B. boys | C. girls | D. dreams
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q10 The word “denied” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______.
A. accepted | B. opposed | C. refused | D. wasted
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. mental health problems | B. unfair environments | C. limited personal growth | D. many talents and ideas
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Gender roles are balanced when women focus on the home and low-paying jobs. | D. Gender inequality still limits opportunities and harms society in many ways.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

541MP/NỀN TẢNG TỪ VỰNG - ĐỌC HIỂU/20 CHỦ ĐỀ TỪ VỰNG VÀ ĐỌC ĐIỀN - ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN/27 Chủ đề 7 Wildlife Conservation and Ecotourism (2).pdfok315 / 0 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:15missing:15
Passage passage-2ccff0fd4d-01-c5d1bb122c (3 questions)
Bali is a popular island in Indonesia. Besides beautiful beaches and culture, it is also known for applying green practices in tourism. In 2019, the local government banned single-use plastic to reduce pollution and protect the ocean. Many hotels and resorts also use eco-friendly materials, recycle waste, and save energy. Tourists in Bali can take part in many green activities. They can visit organic farms, join beach clean-ups, or go on eco-tours to learn about nature and local life. Some diving tours even help protect coral reefs and marine animals. These experiences help travellers enjoy the island while also caring for the environment. Bali is a good example of how tourism and nature can work together. Green tourism not only protects the island’s natural beauty but also supports local people and businesses. Other places can learn from Bali to build a more sustainable and responsible future for travel.
Q1 The word “applying” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by _______.
A. recovering | B. adapting | C. breeding | D. adopting
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q2 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 2 as one of the green activities?
A. join beach clean-ups | B. go on eco-tours | C. learn about local wildlife | D. visit organic farms
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q3 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Bali only focuses on beaches and culture in tourism. | B. The local government in Bali banned single-use plastic in 2022. | C. Tourists in Bali can enjoy the island while still protecting the environment. | D. Green tourism in Bali has reduced the number of tourists visiting the island.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-2ccff0fd4d-02-b786b63fde (4 questions)
The giant panda is one of the most famous and loved animals in the world. It lives mainly in the mountains of Sichuan, China. Known for its black and white fur, the panda has become a symbol of wildlife protection. Although the panda’s population has improved, it is still considered a vulnerable species. The main threat to pandas is habitat loss. As forests are cut down for farming and development, pandas lose their homes and food sources. They rely heavily on bamboo, which only grows in certain areas. Climate change also affects bamboo growth, making survival even harder for the species. To protect pandas, the Chinese government has created large nature reserves. Breeding programs and education efforts have also helped raise awareness. Tourists can visit panda centres to learn more and support conservation. Giant pandas are more than just a cute animal. They represent the need to protect other endangered species and their environments. Saving the panda means saving the forests, which benefits many other animals as well.
Q4 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “Although the panda’s population has improved, it is still considered a vulnerable species.”
A. The number of pandas is growing fast and they are no longer in danger. | B. Even though there are more pandas now, they still face the risk of extinction. | C. Pandas are completely safe thanks to the efforts of the Chinese government. | D. Because their population is high, pandas are not considered vulnerable anymore.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q5 The word “survival” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. development | B. living | C. existence | D. death
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q6 The word “their” in paragraph 4 refers to _______.
A. giant pandas | B. environments | C. the forests | D. other endangered species
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q7 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Giant pandas can survive in any climate as long as bamboo is available. | B. The panda population is growing so fast that it is no longer a concern. | C. The loss of forest land makes it harder for pandas to find food and shelter. | D. Tourists are banned from visiting panda centres to protect the species.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-2ccff0fd4d-03-ef86920626 (8 questions)
Ecotourism is a form of travel that focuses on exploring natural areas while protecting the environment and supporting local communities. Unlike mass tourism, which often leads to pollution and overdevelopment, ecotourism encourages responsible behaviour. In recent years, it has become a growing trend as more travellers seek meaningful experiences and care about nature. People are now more aware of climate change and want to reduce their impact while travelling. Ecotourism offers many different activities. These include hiking through forests, bird-watching, camping in national parks, snorkelling in coral reefs, and visiting wildlife sanctuaries. It also promotes staying in eco-lodges that use renewable energy and local materials. Tourists often take part in environmental education, tree planting, or cultural exchanges with local people. All these activities are designed to be low-impact and educational. A great example of successful ecotourism is Costa Rica. The country protects over 25% of its land through national parks and reserves. Visitors can enjoy rainforests, waterfalls, and volcanoes while learning about biodiversity. Local people benefit from jobs in guiding, hospitality, and conservation projects. However, ecotourism still has some negative sides. If not managed carefully, too many tourists can harm wildlife or damage ecosystems. Some companies may use the term ‘eco’ just for profit. That’s why true ecotourism needs planning, honesty, and long-term thinking.
Q8 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “People are now more aware of climate change and want to reduce their impact while travelling.”
A. Travellers today are less interested in natural beauty and more focused on comfort. | B. More and more tourists now avoid travel completely because of its effect on the climate. | C. Most travellers still ignore climate change when making travel decisions. | D. Travellers today understand climate change better and try to lessen their travel footprint.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q9 The word “It” in paragraph 2 refers to ______.
A. Wildlife | B. Snorkelling | C. Ecotourism | D. Bird-watching
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. threat | B. protection | C. consumption | D. creation
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. eco-lodges | B. rainforests | C. waterfalls | D. volcanoes
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

543MP/NỀN TẢNG TỪ VỰNG - ĐỌC HIỂU/20 CHỦ ĐỀ TỪ VỰNG VÀ ĐỌC ĐIỀN - ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN/30 Chủ đề 8 Inventions (2).pdfok315 / 0 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:15missing:15
Passage passage-b5c28c1b15-01-230718abaf (3 questions)
Driverless cars, powered by advanced sensors and artificial intelligence, promise safer and more efficient transportation. They can reduce accidents caused by human error, follow traffic rules consistently, and select optimal routes to save time and fuel. For elderly or disabled individuals, these vehicles offer greater independence and mobility. Passengers can also use travel time for work, rest, or entertainment instead of focusing on driving. However, driverless cars still face challenges. Their technology is expensive, making them less accessible for the average consumer. Sensors and systems may struggle in extreme weather or unexpected road situations, posing safety risks. Cybersecurity is another concern, as connected vehicles could be vulnerable to hacking. Moreover, widespread adoption could disrupt jobs in the transportation sector. For example, professional drivers could lose their jobs if driverless taxis and trucks become common.
Q1 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the advantages of driverless vehicles?
A. reduce accidents | B. choose the best routes | C. save fuel and money | D. follow traffic regulations
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q2 The word “expensive” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. outdated | B. modern | C. costly | D. affordable
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q3 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Weather conditions have no effect on the performance of driverless cars. | B. Driverless cars cost less than traditional vehicles. | C. Advanced sensors help improve the safety of driverless cars. | D. Professional drivers will not be affected by the rise of driverless vehicles.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-b5c28c1b15-02-b22ca995c3 (4 questions)
Virtual Reality (VR) is a computer-generated simulation that allows users to experience and interact with a three-dimensional environment as if they were physically present. By wearing a VR headset, users can look around, move, and engage with virtual objects in real time. This technology creates an immersive and realistic sense of presence. In education, VR offers students a chance to explore concepts beyond the limits of the classroom. For example, they can take virtual field trips to historical sites, dive into the human body to study anatomy, or conduct science experiments in a safe virtual lab. Such experiences make learning more engaging and help students understand complex ideas through direct interaction. VR can improve retention, enhance motivation, and bridge the gap between theory and practice. It also makes education more accessible, allowing learners from different locations to share the same virtual space. As VR technology becomes more affordable and advanced, its role in education is expected to grow.
Q4 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? “In education, VR offers students a chance to explore concepts beyond the limits of the classroom.”
A. VR allows learners to study ideas and topics outside classroom boundaries. | B. With VR, students’ understanding is confined to the classroom environment. | C. VR replaces all classroom activities by offering only virtual experiences. | D. VR prevents students from interacting with educational content outside school settings.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q5 The word “allows” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by _______.
A. encourages | B. hinders | C. enables | D. hesitates
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q6 The word “It” in paragraph 3 refers to _______.
A. VR | B. retention | C. motivation | D. theory
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q7 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Virtual field trips replace all traditional classroom lessons. | B. Wearing a VR headset lets users interact with objects in real time. | C. VR only helps with science subjects, not history or anatomy. | D. The cost of VR technology is currently too high for schools.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-b5c28c1b15-03-45b3ab6dbe (8 questions)
The earliest stage of mass communication was dominated by print media. Newspapers, magazines, and books allowed information to be recorded, stored, and shared widely. The printing press revolutionised literacy and public awareness, enabling ideas to spread across distances faster than word of mouth. This period laid the foundation for modern journalism and public discourse. The invention of radio and later television transformed communication into a real-time, audio-visual experience. Radio brought news, music, and entertainment into homes instantly, while the other added moving images that engaged audiences more deeply. These media created shared national moments, from presidential speeches to major sporting events, and connected people emotionally. The arrival of the internet marked a turning point, allowing instant global communication. Emails replaced letters, websites offered unlimited information, and online news became available 24/7. Social media began to emerge, giving individuals the power to share their own voices alongside traditional media outlets. Today, interactive platforms dominate. People can stream live events, join online discussions, and access personalised content anytime. Media is no longer just consumed—it is created and shaped by users. This stage continues to evolve, pushing communication toward greater speed, personalisation, and global interconnection.
Q8 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “The earliest stage of mass communication was dominated by print media.”
A. Print media was not widely used during the earliest stage of mass communication. | B. Print media was the primary means of mass communication in its earliest phase. | C. The latter mass communication mostly relied on print media forms. | D. The earliest mass communication happened before print media existed.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q9 The phrase “the other” in paragraph 2 refers to ______.
A. radio | B. television | C. communication | D. experience
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. risk | B. destination | C. advent | D. disappearance
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. vital | B. infinite | C. large | D. restricted
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. joining discussions online | B. accessing personalised content | C. speeding up communication | D. streaming live events
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. People only consume media and do not create content themselves today. | B. Television was the first technology to bring information instantly to homes. | C. Social media existed before the invention of the Internet. | D. Newspapers helped spread information faster than spoken communication.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

545MP/NỀN TẢNG TỪ VỰNG - ĐỌC HIỂU/20 CHỦ ĐỀ TỪ VỰNG VÀ ĐỌC ĐIỀN - ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN/34 Chủ đề 9 Artitificial Intelligence and Technology (2).pdfok315 / 0 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:15missing:15
Passage passage-9bd6d29b8f-01-913f1d6307 (3 questions)
One of the most practical applications of artificial intelligence today is in healthcare. AI-powered systems are able to analyse medical data quickly and suggest accurate diagnoses. For example, image recognition programs can detect signs of cancer in X-rays or MRI scans faster than human doctors. This does not replace medical experts but supports them by reducing mistakes and saving valuable time. As a result, patients can receive treatment earlier, which often improves their chances of recovery. AI is also changing the way hospitals manage their daily work. Smart chatbots can answer patients’ questions, schedule appointments, and remind them to take medicine. At the same time, predictive algorithms help doctors plan treatments based on individual needs. Although there are still challenges, such as privacy and cost, the use of AI in healthcare proves how technology can make medicine more efficient and more human-centred.
Q1 The word “accurate” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______.
A. automated | B. remarkable | C. wrong | D. precise
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q2 According to paragraph 2, AI helps hospitals manage work by all of the following, EXCEPT _______.
A. scheduling appointments | B. answering patients’ questions | C. cutting costs | D. planning treatments
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q3 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. AI completely replaces doctors in diagnosing diseases. | B. AI can only be used in hospitals for scheduling appointments. | C. Image recognition programs can detect cancer signs faster than doctors. | D. Privacy and cost are no longer concerns in applying AI to healthcare.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-9bd6d29b8f-02-33814de19c (4 questions)
Modern agriculture is no longer only about manual labour and traditional methods. Farmers today rely on advanced machines, data analysis, and digital platforms to increase productivity. Smart systems monitor soil quality, control irrigation, and predict weather patterns more accurately than humans. By combining technology with farming, agriculture becomes more sustainable and efficient. One of the most useful innovations is precision farming. Sensors placed in the field measure temperature, humidity, and nutrient levels, while drones capture images of crops from above. These tools help farmers decide when to water, fertilise, or harvest. Artificial intelligence supports this process by turning raw data into clear suggestions, saving time and resources while improving crop yield. The benefits go beyond higher production. Smarter farming methods also reduce waste, use less water, and limit harmful chemicals, which protects the environment. In developing countries, such technology can help communities fight food shortages and adapt to climate change. Although AI is important, the main goal remains to build a stronger and more sustainable agricultural system for the future.
Q4 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “By combining technology with farming, agriculture becomes more sustainable and efficient.”
A. Mixing farming with technology increases sustainability but lowers productivity. | B. The use of technology in farming helps improve efficiency but reduces sustainability. | C. Applying technology in farming makes agriculture more eco-friendly and productive. | D. Technology in agriculture keeps it sustainable while maintaining the same efficiency.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q5 The phrase “These tools” in paragraph 2 refers to _______.
A. useful innovations | B. images of crops | C. nutrient levels | D. sensors and drones
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q6 The word “shortages” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. abundances | B. limitations | C. abilities | D. products
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q7 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Modern agriculture depends largely on manual labour and traditional methods. | B. Precision farming uses sensors and drones to help improve crop yield. | C. Farmers use drones only to water their crops. | D. The main goal of AI in farming is to replace farmers and make it sustainable.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-9bd6d29b8f-03-f57e4974ff (8 questions)
An AI chatbot is a computer program designed to simulate human conversation through text or voice. Using natural language processing and machine learning, it can understand questions, provide answers, and even maintain long discussions. Chatbots are now integrated into websites, messaging apps, and digital platforms, making communication between humans and machines more natural and accessible. The most obvious advantage of this technology is efficiency. In customer service, a chatbot can respond instantly to thousands of users at the same time, solving basic problems without human staff. In education, it supports students with explanations and practice tasks. Even in daily life, people rely on chatbots for translation, scheduling, or quick information. This makes services faster, cheaper, and more convenient. However, chatbots are far from perfect. They sometimes misunderstand context, provide inaccurate information, or sound unnatural in sensitive conversations. Unlike humans, they cannot show genuine empathy or creativity. Overreliance on pre-trained data also limits their ability to deal with unexpected questions. These weaknesses remind us that chatbots are tools, not replacements for human intelligence. A more serious problem is overuse or misuse. Some people depend too heavily on chatbots for homework, writing, or decision-making, which may reduce critical thinking skills. Others worry about “brainwashing,” since users can be influenced by biased or manipulated responses. Therefore, while chatbots bring many benefits, society must use them responsibly to avoid dependency and maintain human judgment.
Q8 The word “it” in paragraph 1 refers to ______.
A. machine learning | B. an AI chatbot | C. text or voice | D. human conversation
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q9 Which of the following is NOT mentioned as one of the abilities of AI chatbots in paragraph 1?
A. learn foreign languages | B. keep long discussions | C. give answers | D. understand questions
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q10 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 3? “Overreliance on pre-trained data also limits their ability to deal with unexpected questions.”
A. Relying too much on pre-trained data makes them less effective with unfamiliar questions. | B. Heavy use of pre-trained data makes chatbots better at managing predictable data. | C. Depending on pre-trained data allows them to answer unexpected questions more accurately. | D. Reliance on data improves efficiency but weakens responses to unfamiliar questions.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. fair | B. wrong | C. right | D. unequal
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

547MP/NỀN TẢNG TỪ VỰNG - ĐỌC HIỂU/20 CHỦ ĐỀ TỪ VỰNG VÀ ĐỌC ĐIỀN - ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN/38 Chủ đề 10 Cities and Urbanisation (2).pdfok315 / 0 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:15missing:15
Passage passage-6738488a5d-01-ac08bf785c (3 questions)
Urbanisation in Australia has developed rapidly, especially in coastal areas where most major cities are located. Sydney, Melbourne, and Brisbane have grown into large urban centres that attract people from all over the world. These cities are known for their multicultural communities, modern architecture, and strong economies. Unlike many countries, urban growth in Australia is often planned with great attention to quality of life, which makes its cities both vibrant and welcoming. What is interesting about urbanisation in Australia is the way cities combine modern life with nature. For example, residents in Sydney can work in tall office buildings in the morning and relax on Bondi Beach in the afternoon. Green parks, clean air, and open spaces are carefully protected, even as the population grows. This balance between development and nature makes Australian cities unique examples of sustainable urban living.
Q1 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the aspects making the three cities known?
A. modern architecture | B. multiculturalism | C. developed economies | D. major coastal areas
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q2 The word “combine” in paragraph 2 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______.
A. integrate | B. construct | C. replace | D. decide
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q3 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Urbanisation in Australia happens mostly in inland areas. | B. Sydney, Melbourne, and Brisbane are small cities with little international influence. | C. Australian cities aim to balance modern development with nature. | D. Green spaces are often destroyed to make room for tall buildings.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-6738488a5d-02-9300483893 (4 questions)
A city often whispers of opportunities: the chance to study in universities that shape the future, or to walk into hospitals where the latest technology can save lives. Therefore, people have always been drawn to cities, not only because they are larger and brighter, but also because they seem to promise something more. For many families, these possibilities feel like doors opening to a better tomorrow. Work, too, pulls people into the urban rhythm. In cities, offices light up late into the night, factories hum with energy, and cafés are full of quiet conversations about new projects. In cities, jobs are not just about survival; they offer a path to ambition. The chance to earn more, to dream bigger, becomes part of everyday life. And beyond work and study, cities hold a special charm in their culture. A theatre performance, a street festival, a late-night stroll through neon markets—these moments make life richer. People from different places meet, share stories, and create communities that could never exist in isolation. In this way, cities become more than concrete and glass; they become living spaces where energy, diversity, and creativity thrive.
Q4 The word “they” in paragraph 1 refers to _______.
A. cities | B. people | C. lives | D. universities
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q5 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? “In cities, jobs are not just about survival; they offer a path to ambition.”
A. City jobs mainly help people cover their daily living expenses. | B. Work in cities provides opportunities for people to achieve higher goals. | C. Employment in cities ensures survival without room for bigger aspirations. | D. Jobs in cities ensure both living and the possibility to aim for more.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q6 The word “isolation” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. loneliness | B. limitation | C. separation | D. contact
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q7 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. People move to cities only because they are larger and brighter. | B. Cities provide opportunities for education, work, and cultural experiences. | C. Jobs in cities are focused only on survival, not ambition. | D. Urban life prevents people from building diverse communities.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-6738488a5d-03-07f3bb7389 (8 questions)
Urbanisation is the shift of people from rural areas into cities, and with that movement comes both hope and challenge. Cities rise higher, roads stretch farther, and lights burn brighter, but behind these changes lie questions about how people will live together in limited space. For many, urbanisation has been a symbol of progress. In Australia, for example, cities such as Melbourne and Sydney have become centres of opportunity where education, healthcare, and employment are within reach. Families who once lived far from schools or hospitals now find themselves surrounded by services that can transform their lives. In this sense, cities are magnets, drawing people with the promise of higher living standards and more diverse experiences. Yet urbanisation also reveals its darker side. The same growth that fills skylines with glass towers can also create slums at their base. Dhaka, one of the most densely populated cities in the world, struggles daily with overcrowding, pollution, and shortages of housing. Traffic jams steal hours from workers, and rising living costs make survival difficult for the poor. Without balance, the dream of the city easily turns into frustration. That is why planning becomes so important. If governments invest in affordable housing, efficient transport, and green spaces, cities can grow without suffocating their people. Sustainable design, like Singapore’s vertical gardens or Copenhagen’s cycling culture, shows that urbanisation does not need to destroy quality of life. Instead, with thoughtful planning, it can create places where ambition and well- being walk hand in hand.
Q8 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “Urbanisation is the shift of people from rural areas into cities, and with that movement comes both hope and challenge.”
A. Urbanisation refers to people being forced to abandon city life and return permanently to the rural areas | B. Urbanisation means that cities expand into rural lands and, in doing so, remove every problem that | C. Urbanisation is a force that provides only advantages for people and never creates any social or | D. Urbanisation is the process of people moving from the countryside into cities, bringing both
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

550MP/NỀN TẢNG TỪ VỰNG - ĐỌC HIỂU/20 CHỦ ĐỀ TỪ VỰNG VÀ ĐỌC ĐIỀN - ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN/Chủ đề 2 Health and Well-Being (2).pdfok315 / 0 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:15missing:15
Passage passage-3c77b8bd37-01-3c6d396a9b (3 questions)
Many people believe that exercise alone can help them lose weight, but that is a common misunderstanding. Exercise supports weight loss, but it cannot replace a healthy diet. Physical activities like walking, running, or swimming help burn calories, build muscles, and keep the body strong. However, without eating properly, exercise won't be very effective. For best results, people should combine regular workouts with a healthy eating plan. A balanced diet plays the most important role in weight loss. It means eating the right kinds of food in the right amounts. A good diet includes vegetables, fruits, whole grains, lean proteins, and healthy fats. Avoiding fast food, sugary snacks, and soft drinks is also necessary. When people eat healthy and control their portions, their bodies receive the nutrients they need without extra calories. This helps reduce fat and improve overall health.
Q1 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the benefits of physical activities?
A. burn calories | B. replace a healthy diet | C. build muscles | D. keep the body strong
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q2 The word “supports” in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. prevent | B. assist | C. respect | D. improve
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q3 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Exercise is more important than eating healthy for losing weight. | B. People can lose weight by exercising alone without changing their diet. | C. A combination of regular exercise and a balanced diet is best for weight loss. | D. Eating fast food in small amounts is encouraged for a healthy lifestyle.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-3c77b8bd37-02-ccd5114419 (4 questions)
Lack of sleep is a serious problem that affects both physical and mental health. When people don’t get enough rest, their bodies cannot recover properly. This can lead to headaches, tiredness, and a weakened immune system, making it easier to get sick. In addition to physical health, sleep deprivation also harms mental performance. It becomes harder to concentrate, remember things, and make good decisions. Students who do not sleep enough may find it difficult to study and perform poorly on exams. Over time, it can even lead to stress, anxiety, or depression. Lack of sleep causes many troubles in daily life. People who don’t sleep enough often feel tired and have no energy to do their tasks. They may forget important things or make mistakes at work or school. Sleepy people also get angry or upset more easily, which can lead to problems in relationships. In serious cases, lack of sleep can cause accidents, especially when driving or using machines. That is why getting enough rest every night is very important.
Q4 The word “weakened” in paragraph 1 is opposite in meaning to _______.
A. famous | B. important | C. crowded | D. enhanced
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q5 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 2 as one of the mental problems caused by a lack of sleep?
A. stress | B. depression | C. tiredness | D. anxiety
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q6 The word “They” in paragraph 3 refers to _______.
A. Many troubles | B. People | C. Tasks | D. Important things
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q7 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Sleep deprivation improves students’ performance on exams. | B. People who don’t sleep enough may have problems in their relationships. | C. Lack of sleep makes the immune system stronger. | D. Sleepy people are more likely to stay focused and make better decisions.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-3c77b8bd37-03-6e28b93589 (8 questions)
Being healthy does not only mean having a strong body but also having a clear and positive mind. Both physical and mental health are important, and they work together to help us live well, study better, and enjoy life. Good physical health means your body is working properly. To stay physically healthy, people should eat a balanced diet, drink enough water, get regular exercise, and sleep well. It is also important to have regular health check-ups. These habits help the body grow stronger, prevent diseases, and give us energy for daily activities. Mental health is about our thoughts, feelings, and how we handle stress. Good mental health means feeling calm, confident, and in control. People can protect their mental health by talking to others, relaxing, avoiding too much stress, and doing things they enjoy. Sometimes, even small things like listening to music or walking in nature can improve your mood. In conclusion, both physical and mental health are important. If you are physically strong but feel sad or stressed all the time, you cannot enjoy life. On the other hand, if your mind is happy but your body is weak or sick, you may not have the energy to do anything. So, we must take care of both to live a full and healthy life.
Q8 The word “regular” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______.
A. huge | B. hard | C. easy | D. rare
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

552MP/NỀN TẢNG TỪ VỰNG - ĐỌC HIỂU/20 CHỦ ĐỀ TỪ VỰNG VÀ ĐỌC ĐIỀN - ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN/Chủ đề 3 Education and Learning (2).pdfok315 / 0 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:15missing:15
Passage passage-8c3c45f200-01-64cac30e33 (3 questions)
After high school, students can choose between vocational training and higher education. Vocational programs often emphasise practical skills related to specific occupations, such as automotive repair, beauty services, or hospitality. These courses are usually shorter but may lead to faster employment. For instance, a student completing a two-year cooking course might start working in a restaurant right away. In some industries, vocational graduates are in high demand due to their specialised skills. University education, on the other hand, offers broader knowledge in fields like science, business, or the humanities. It covers a wide range of subjects, from economics to social sciences, and may offer broader career pathways. Some jobs, like teaching or engineering, often require a university qualification. However, the academic route may also involve higher costs and longer study periods. The decision between these two options can depend on learning styles, personal interests, and long-term career planning.
Q1 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the examples of specific occupations?
A. hospitality | B. beauty services | C. humanities | D. automotive repair
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. includes | B. widens | C. approaches | D. improves
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q3 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Vocational training always takes longer than university education. | B. University graduates are more in demand than vocational graduates in all industries. | C. Vocational programs often lead to faster employment. | D. University education is cheap and suitable for everyone, regardless of career goals.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-8c3c45f200-02-2ed576a17b (4 questions)
In Vietnam, the university entrance exam plays an important role in the education system. Each year, almost all students take this exam after finishing Grade 12. It is often considered a major turning point, as the results decide which universities or colleges students can attend. The pressure surrounding the exam is high, with months of preparation, extra classes, and practice tests. The exam usually covers core subjects like Math, Literature, English, and sometimes Science or Social Studies, depending on the student’s chosen group. The format combines multiple-choice questions and written responses. The exam aims to ensure fairness and standardisation. However, some argue that it focuses too much on memorisation rather than critical thinking or creativity. Recently, some changes have been introduced to improve the process. Universities are allowed to use multiple admission methods, including high school GPA and aptitude tests. However, the traditional exam remains central for most students. The intense competition and high expectations continue to make it one of the most challenging experiences in Vietnamese student life.
Q4 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? Each year, almost all students take this exam after finishing Grade 12.
A. Nearly all students sit the exam after completing Grade 12 every year. | B. Students take the exam many times before they finish Grade 12. | C. Only a small number of students take the exam after Grade 12. | D. Students are required to take the exam to graduate from high school.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q5 The word it in paragraph 2 refers to _______.
A. fairness | B. the exam | C. standardisation | D. memorisation
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. dangerous | B. complex | C. difficult | D. easy
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q7 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. All students are accepted into universities without taking any exam. | B. The entrance exam in Vietnam no longer plays a major role in university admission. | C. The exam system has completely changed to focus on creativity. | D. Many students experience pressure and spend months preparing for the exam.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-8c3c45f200-03-6c6ffb375a (8 questions)
Online learning has become more popular in recent years, especially after the pandemic. It allows students to attend classes from anywhere, saving time and travel costs. Online platforms often provide recorded lessons, which help learners review at their own pace. This method also supports flexible schedules, which is useful for those with other commitments. However, online learning has several drawbacks. Students may find it harder to focus without a classroom setting. Technical issues, such as unstable internet or device problems, can interrupt lessons. In addition, a lack of face-to-face contact may reduce motivation and make it difficult to ask questions or join discussions actively. In contrast, traditional learning provides a structured environment. Teachers and students interact directly, which helps build relationships and encourages immediate feedback. Group work and in-class activities often improve communication and teamwork skills. For many learners, this setting creates a stronger sense of discipline and responsibility. Still, face-to-face learning also presents challenges. Fixed timetables may not suit students with part-time jobs or other duties. Long commutes can be tiring and reduce personal study time. Some students feel anxious in large classes or hesitate to speak up in front of others. Physical classrooms may also limit access to multimedia tools or global resources available online.

A. reducing | B. tracking | C. slowing | D. wasting
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. platforms | B. learners | C. travel costs | D. recorded lessons
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. access | B. distract | C. consider | D. concentrate
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. technical issues | B. difficulty focusing | C. joining discussions actively | D. a lack of face-to-face contact
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

555MP/NỀN TẢNG TỪ VỰNG - ĐỌC HIỂU/20 CHỦ ĐỀ TỪ VỰNG VÀ ĐỌC ĐIỀN - ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN/Chủ đề 6 The Environment (2).pdfok315 / 0 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:15missing:15
Passage passage-52239021b2-01-c36b9300a1 (3 questions)
For decades, Japan’s coastal waters were rich in fish, such as tuna, sardines, and mackerel, supporting both local diets and a massive fishing industry. However, due to unsustainable fishing practices and high consumer demand, fish stocks have declined sharply. Bluefin tuna populations, for example, have dropped to critical levels. This threatens biodiversity and the livelihoods of small-scale fishermen. To address this issue, the Japanese government has implemented fishing quotas, seasonal bans, and promoted aquaculture as an alternative. While these measures have shown some positive effects, recovery is slow and uncertain. The situation in Japan highlights the long-term risks of depleting natural resources for short-term gain. Without strict regulation and global cooperation, overfishing may push several marine species toward extinction and destabilise ocean ecosystems permanently.
Q1 The word “rich” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by _______.
A. important | B. huge | C. lucky | D. abundant
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q2 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 2 as one of the Japanese government's efforts towards addressing overfishing?
A. seasonal bans | B. fishing quotas | C. global cooperation | D. aquaculture promotion
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q3 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Japan's fish population has remained stable due to advanced fishing methods. | B. Aquaculture has replaced traditional fishing across all of Japan. | C. The Japanese government has done nothing to stop overfishing. | D. Poor fishing practices have led to a sharp decline in bluefin tuna populations.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-52239021b2-02-6b2dae331f (4 questions)
The first “R” in the 3Rs stands for Reduce, which means cutting down on the amount of waste we produce. This can be done by avoiding unnecessary packaging, choosing products with a longer lifespan, and limiting single-use items. Reducing consumption at the source not only saves natural resources but also lessens the burden on landfills and the environment. The second “R” is Reuse, which encourages people to use items multiple times before throwing them away. Reusable shopping bags, containers, and glass bottles are examples of how we can extend the life of everyday products. Even old clothes and furniture can be repurposed or donated. Reusing helps reduce demand for new goods and lowers production-related emissions. The third “R” refers to Recycle, which involves turning used materials into new products. Paper, plastic, metal, and glass can all be collected and recycled into reusable materials. Recycling conserves resources and reduces energy usage compared to making products from raw materials. However, it should be the last option after reducing and reusing.
Q4 The word “lessens” in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. values | B. minimises | C. declines | D. increases
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q5 The word “them” in paragraph 2 refers to _______.
A. people | B. items | C. shopping bags | D. multiple times
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q6 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? “Reusing helps reduce demand for new goods and lowers production-related emissions.”
A. Reusing stops factories from making new products and reduces energy use. | B. Using items more than once cuts demand and lowers emissions from production. | C. Buying second-hand goods makes companies produce more and pollute less. | D. Reusing things increases the number of goods made and waste produced.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q7 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Recycling is more important than reducing and reusing in waste management. | B. Reuse involves turning used materials into something entirely new again. | C. Reducing waste helps save resources and ease pressure on the environment. | D. The 3Rs promote buying more single-use products to help the economy.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-52239021b2-03-4b571ce822 (8 questions)
While many people focus on emissions from transport and industry, agricultural practices also contribute significantly to climate change. Farming, especially large-scale animal agriculture, plays a major role in global warming and the increase of greenhouse gases. It’s essential to know about this connection to make good decisions about food consumption. One key issue is that livestock farming produces large amounts of methane. It is a powerful greenhouse gas released through animal digestion and manure. In addition, deforestation for grazing land reduces the planet’s ability to absorb carbon dioxide. Fertilisers used in crop farming also emit nitrous oxide, another potent greenhouse gas. Together, these factors make agriculture a major contributor to rising global temperatures. To address the problem, some countries are promoting sustainable farming techniques. These methods include improving animal diets to reduce methane, using organic fertilisers, and rotating crops or growing trees alongside crops. New technologies are also being tested, such as special feed that helps animals release less methane and growing food in tall indoor buildings, known as vertical farms. Ultimately, individual choices matter. Reducing meat consumption – especially beef – can lower demand for resource-intensive livestock farming. Instead, choosing fish, seafood, or plant-based meals helps reduce one’s carbon footprint and supports a more climate-friendly food system.
Q8 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “It’s essential to know about this connection to make good decisions about food consumption.”
A. Making food choices without knowledge can lead to poor long-term decisions. | B. Understanding nutrition is vital to eating the right amount of food each day. | C. Knowing the link helps people make smarter choices about what they eat. | D. People should always base their diet only on personal taste and convenience.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. captures | B. digests | C. consumes | D. generates
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. capture | B. create | C. discharge | D. refuse
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

558MP/NỀN TẢNG TỪ VỰNG - ĐỌC HIỂU/20 CHỦ ĐỀ TỪ VỰNG VÀ ĐỌC ĐIỀN - ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN/Chủ đề 11 The Mass Media (2).pdfok315 / 0 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:15missing:15
Passage passage-1e7ec6a755-01-66383302fd (3 questions)
Social media offers young people countless chances to connect with others and expand their knowledge. With just a few clicks, they can keep in touch with friends, build new relationships, and join online communities that share similar interests. At the same time, social media provides access to news, educational videos, and tutorials that help students learn new skills, improve their studies, and stay updated with world events. These opportunities make learning faster, more creative, and more engaging. Another benefit is that social media gives young people a stage to express their creativity. They can share artwork, music, or writing and receive feedback from a global audience. Many even find career opportunities through networking sites or by promoting their talents online. In addition, social media inspires self-confidence and encourages young users to explore new ideas, turning it into a valuable tool for personal growth and future success.
Q1 According to paragraph 1, social media enables access to all of the following, EXCEPT _______.
A. tutorials | B. news | C. online courses | D. educational videos
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. allowing | B. encouraging | C. replacing | D. advertising
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q3 Which of the following is NOT true according to the passage?
A. Social media helps young people connect with friends and communities. | B. Social media distracts students from their studies but still offers learning benefits. | C. Young people can showcase creativity and gain feedback through social media. | D. Some users may find career opportunities by promoting their talents online.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-1e7ec6a755-02-d91e3050eb (4 questions)
In the past, mass media were mainly limited to newspapers, magazines, and radio. People waited for the morning paper to read the latest news, while radio programs entertained families and informed them about national events. Although slow compared to today’s standards, these media were trusted and respected. Later, television became the most influential type of mass media. With moving images and sound, it brought the world into people’s living rooms. It provided not only news but also entertainment, education, and advertisements. For several decades, television shaped people’s opinions, habits, and even fashion. This period marked a turning point when mass media became more visual and powerful. Today, the rise of the internet and smartphones has transformed mass media once again. Online newspapers, social networks, and streaming services allow information to travel instantly. Unlike traditional media, digital platforms are interactive, meaning users can comment, share, and create their own content. While convenient and fast, modern mass media also present challenges such as fake news and information overload.
Q4 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “Although slow compared to today’s standards, these media were trusted and respected.”
A. Old forms of mass media delivered information quickly but were unreliable. | B. These media were slower than today’s, but considered reliable and credible. | C. Modern mass media are faster and more trustworthy than these forms of media. | D. Newspapers and radio spread news instantly and earned little public respect.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q5 The word “it” in paragraph 2 refers to _______.
A. sound | B. television | C. type | D. mass media
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q6 The word “rise” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. purpose | B. limit | C. trend | D. drop
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q7 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. In the past, people received news instantly through newspapers and the radio. | B. Television had little effect on people’s opinions, habits, or fashion. | C. Digital platforms allow users to comment, share, and create their own content. | D. Modern mass media are slower and less interactive than traditional media.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-1e7ec6a755-03-504f25ff25 (8 questions)
Fake news refers to false or misleading information that is created and shared as if it were true. Unlike honest mistakes in reporting, fake news is often produced on purpose to attract attention, influence opinions, or earn money through advertising. With the rapid growth of social media, people can now access, believe, and spread such stories more easily than ever before. The impact of fake news is serious and far-reaching. It confuses readers, damages trust in journalism, and sometimes creates fear or anger in society. Fake news can also harm individuals or organisations by spreading lies that destroy reputations. Furthermore, when people cannot distinguish truth from fiction, their personal choices and social decisions become less reliable, leading to instability. During elections, fake news is often used to influence voters. For instance, certain online pages may publish exaggerated or completely false claims about a candidate’s background or policy. Another common case is health-related fake news, such as unproven cures for serious diseases or dangerous diets. Many people believe and share these stories without fact-checking, and the consequences can be both harmful and long-lasting. To reduce fake news, individuals should practice critical thinking and verify information before sharing it with others. Schools should teach students media literacy so they learn to question online content. Above all, people must rely on trustworthy sources and support reliable journalism to protect society from misinformation.
Q8 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the purposes of creating fake news?
A. deceive people | B. attract attention | C. earn money | D. affect opinions
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q9 The word “their” in paragraph 2 refers to ______.
A. reputations | B. lies | C. people | D. personal choices
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q10 The word “confuses” in paragraph 2 can be best replaced by ______.
A. reduces | B. simplifies | C. bewilders | D. fosters
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. announce | B. report | C. launch | D. conceal
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Schools must prevent students from reading news and articles on the internet. | D. Students should be taught media literacy at schools to evaluate online information.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Passage passage-6be51a7e12-01-cae5abcc0c (3 questions)
The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation has become one of the most influential charitable organisations in the world. Its projects reach across continents, focusing on healthcare, vaccines, and the prevention of deadly diseases. By funding research and working with local health systems, the foundation helps communities reduce the risks of malaria, polio, and other preventable illnesses. The effort is not only about medicine but also about building stronger systems that give people a chance to live healthier lives. Education and poverty alleviation form another major part of its mission. Scholarships are offered to students who might otherwise lose opportunities, and resources are directed to schools in low-income regions. Farmers in developing countries receive tools, seeds, and training so they can feed their families and sell crops in local markets. With science, innovation, and long-term commitment, the foundation shapes opportunities where they once seemed impossible.
Q1 The word “influential” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______.
A. generous | B. positive | C. productive | D. impactful
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q2 The word “their” in paragraph 2 refers to _______.
A. farmers | B. tools | C. seeds | D. developing countries
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation only provides medicine to cure diseases. | B. Education is also part of the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation’s mission. | C. Farmers in developing countries are given food by the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation. | D. Scholarships from the foundation are limited to students in rich nations.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-6be51a7e12-02-892e0c570a (4 questions)
In many societies, men and women have the same potential but are not always treated equally in the workplace. Women may face barriers such as lower pay, fewer chances of promotion, or stereotypes about their abilities. Men can also be pressured by traditional roles that limit their choices. Equal job opportunities mean removing these obstacles so that everyone can develop their skills and talents without discrimination. The benefits of equal opportunities are seen in both economic growth and social progress. When women have the chance to lead, companies gain new perspectives and innovative solutions. When men are allowed to share household duties, women can pursue careers more freely. Communities become stronger when all people can contribute according to their abilities, rather than being limited by outdated expectations. Governments and organisations play an important role in creating this fairness. Laws that protect against gender bias in hiring, salaries, and promotion are essential. Schools and universities prepare young people by teaching them about gender equality, so future employees and employers can approach work with open minds.
Q4 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “Men can also be pressured by traditional roles that limit their choices.”
A. Traditional expectations may restrict men’s freedom to choose from different life options. | B. Men are likely to face pressure from conventional roles that reduce their opportunities. | C. Family duties never allow men to explore wider opportunities without social limitations. | D. Stereotypes usually give men unlimited choices and greater freedom in their careers.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. ancient | B. well-known | C. ordinary | D. modern
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. promotion | B. teaching | C. recruitment | D. salaries
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. Without government support, achieving gender fairness in workplaces is impossible. | B. Women leaders give companies new perspectives and provide creative solutions. | C. Only women benefit from equal job opportunities, while men remain unaffected. | D. Laws on pay and promotion are unnecessary as gender bias is rare today.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-6be51a7e12-03-b2de8b7236 (8 questions)
Small acts of kindness can often make a big difference in someone’s life. The idea of “Pay It Forward” encourages people to help others without expecting anything in return. Instead, the person who receives kindness is inspired to pass it on to someone else. This simple chain reaction has the power to create positive energy in families, schools, and even entire communities. Many campaigns have been launched to spread this spirit. For example, some restaurants invite customers to buy an extra meal for a stranger in need. In schools, students might share stationery, tutor a classmate, or simply offer kind words of encouragement. Social media has also played a role in expanding the reach of “Pay It Forward.” Stories and videos of kind acts inspire millions of people to join the movement. Seeing how one person’s generosity can lift another person’s spirit motivates others to take similar action. The campaigns remind society that kindness is contagious when people choose to care. Beyond individual actions, organisations and companies use these campaigns to strengthen connections with communities. Employees may volunteer their time, donate supplies, or organise charity drives. These activities not only support people in need but also build stronger relationships among colleagues. By encouraging everyone to participate, “Pay It Forward” turns kindness into a shared responsibility and a habit that grows naturally over time.
Q8 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “The idea of “Pay It Forward” encourages people to help others without expecting anything in return.”
A. “Pay It Forward” teaches that kindness should always bring rewards to the giver. | B. According to “Pay It Forward,” kindness must be exchanged equally between people. | C. “Pay It Forward” advises communities not to help strangers in daily situations. | D. Through “Pay It Forward,” people are urged to help others without expecting rewards.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. reach | B. suppress | C. extend | D. pursue
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. curious | B. purposeful | C. vital | D. mutual
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. restaurants | B. companies | C. stations | D. social media
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Passage passage-e24c171741-01-bcb2c6c743 (3 questions)
Nikola Tesla was more than an inventor; he was a visionary who imagined a world powered by clean and limitless energy. Born in 1856, Tesla contributed ground-breaking ideas such as alternating current (AC), which made long-distance electricity transmission possible and changed the way people lived. His experiments with wireless power and renewable sources reflected his dream of providing free energy for all humanity. At a time when others focused only on profit, Tesla looked ahead to a future where technology could uplift societies and reduce inequality. Although many of his ambitious projects were never completed, Tesla’s vision continues to inspire modern scientists and engineers. Today, discussions about sustainable energy, wireless charging, and green technology often echo Tesla’s early ideas. He proved that daring to think differently can shape the progress of generations. Remembering Tesla reminds us that true innovation lies not only in invention but also in the courage to dream beyond limits.
Q1 The word “ground-breaking” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______.
A. significant | B. optimistic | C. innovative | D. impactful
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q2 According to paragraph 2, Tesla's ideas spark discussions about all of the following, EXCEPT ________.
A. green tech | B. wireless devices | C. cordless charging | D. sustainable energy
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q3 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Tesla focused mainly on making profits from his inventions and ideas. | B. Most of Tesla’s projects were completed successfully during his lifetime. | C. Tesla’s vision of clean energy still inspires modern science and technology. | D. Tesla believed that innovation should be limited rather than dreaming too high.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-e24c171741-02-e3e3ca7a0d (4 questions)
Born in 1847 in Ohio, Thomas Edison showed curiosity from a young age. He often dismantled household items to see how they worked. His interest in mechanics and electricity grew stronger when he worked as a telegraph operator, a job that exposed him to the practical use of electrical signals and sparked ideas for his future inventions. In his laboratory in Menlo Park, Edison gathered a team of skilled assistants and devoted himself to experiments. He pursued improvements in the telegraph and telephone before moving on to the electric light. The challenge of finding the right filament for the light bulb required extraordinary persistence. Reports say he tested thousands of materials – cotton, bamboo, and even hair – before discovering a carbonised filament that glowed steadily without burning out too quickly. His determination extended to other fields as well. Edison developed the phonograph, allowing sound to be recorded and played back for the first time. He also worked on motion picture cameras, helping to open a new era of entertainment. By the end of his life, he held more than a thousand patents. Each of them was the result of countless trials and an unshaken commitment to experimentation.
Q4 The word “dismantled” in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. constructed | B. opened | C. destroyed | D. assembled
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. He first worked to invent the telegraph and telephone, then turned to the light bulb. | B. Before focusing on the electric light, he tried to enhance the telegraph and telephone. | C. He concentrated on the light bulb, ignoring devices like the telegraph and telephone. | D. He improved the telegraph and telephone after he finished creating the electric light.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q6 The word “them” in paragraph 3 refers to _______.
A. patents | B. cameras | C. other fields | D. countless trials
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Edison found the best light bulb filament after testing only a few common materials. | D. Motion picture technology developed without any contribution from Edison’s research.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-e24c171741-03-6d45bb7f4f (8 questions)
History offers figures whose lives carry lessons of endurance and vision, and from them we can draw deep inspiration. The point is not to idealise them but to examine how their decisions and sacrifices in difficult times created possibilities for future generations. Abraham Lincoln stands as one such figure. Rising from poverty to the presidency, he faced the moral and political crisis of slavery in America. His choice to preserve the Union while issuing the Emancipation Proclamation required immense courage. What inspires us is not merely that he signed a document, but that he held steady through years of war, criticism, and personal loss. Another striking example is Marie Curie. At a time when women were excluded from most scientific circles, she worked tirelessly in small laboratories, isolating radium and polonium. The physical toll of handling radioactive materials and the scepticism of her peers did not deter her. Her double Nobel Prizes testify not just to scientific discovery but to relentless devotion. Inspiration comes from imagining the cold nights in her lab, the uncertainty of results, and her decision to continue regardless. To take inspiration seriously means going beyond names and titles. When we analyse Lincoln’s endurance or Curie’s devotion, we encounter human qualities that demand reflection: moral courage, intellectual rigour, and sacrifice. These are not distant ideals but living patterns, offering us pathways when we face our own complex struggles.
Q8 The word “them” in paragraph 1 refers to ______.
A. figures | B. lives | C. lessons | D. endurance and vision
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. candidates | B. purposes | C. opportunities | D. abilities
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

564MP/NỀN TẢNG TỪ VỰNG - ĐỌC HIỂU/20 CHỦ ĐỀ TỪ VỰNG VÀ ĐỌC ĐIỀN - ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN/Chủ đề 14 Becoming Independent (2).pdfok315 / 0 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:15missing:15
Passage passage-5e8d61b776-01-b45882b7c9 (3 questions)
Time management is the ability to organize activities within limited hours of the day. It involves setting priorities, planning schedules, and using time effectively so that important tasks are completed on time. Students, workers, and even parents all need this skill because daily life brings more responsibilities than people can handle without planning. With good time management, individuals avoid wasting hours on unnecessary activities and keep their routines under control. For university students, time management means balancing lectures, assignments, and part-time jobs without feeling overwhelmed. They may use calendars, to-do lists, or digital apps to track deadlines. At the same time, breaks for rest, meals, and exercise must also be included. Learning how to control time in this way not only supports academic success but also creates healthy habits that make daily challenges easier to face.
Q1 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the things related to time management?
A. giving priorities | B. schedule planning | C. wasting time | D. good use of time
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. struggles | B. benefits | C. conditions | D. impacts
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. Time management means focusing only on work tasks while ignoring personal needs. | B. Students practice time management by studying continuously without taking breaks. | C. Calendars, to-do lists, or apps can help students balance deadlines and activities. | D. Good time management requires avoiding schedules because they reduce daily flexibility.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-5e8d61b776-02-55681d2529 (4 questions)
Helping children become independent is an important task for both families and schools. Independence does not appear suddenly but develops gradually through daily habits and experiences. When children are encouraged to make choices and take responsibility, they grow more confident in their abilities. One useful way is giving children simple duties such as tidying their room, setting the table, or helping with simple cooking. These tasks train discipline and teach that every action has consequences. Parents can also allow children to decide on small matters like choosing clothes or planning weekend activities. At school, teachers may guide students to practice self-study so they know how to review lessons without waiting for instructions. Children should also learn basic skills such as handling money, organizing schedules, and solving simple problems. When mistakes happen, adults need to provide gentle guidance instead of doing everything for children. Gradually, the experience of managing responsibilities helps young people gain confidence and maturity, making independence a natural part of their growth.
Q4 The word “they” in paragraph 1 refers to _______.
A. experiences | B. daily habits | C. children | D. choices
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. individual | B. effortless | C. complex | D. easy
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Parents should ignore mistakes completely so children can solve all issues alone. | D. Adults should guide kids kindly through mistakes rather than fixing every problem.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Passage passage-5e8d61b776-03-4d63682baa (8 questions)
When students enter university, many of them decide to take a part-time job. The reason is often financial pressure, since they must pay for rent, meals, and study materials without relying completely on their families. Besides money, students also want practical experience that cannot be gained from lectures alone. Part-time jobs provide clear benefits. Students can earn income to support their basic needs and even save for emergencies. The workplace also teaches valuable life skills, such as communication, teamwork, and problem-solving. Confidence grows when they deal with customers or supervisors. In addition, these experiences make their résumés stronger and prepare them better for full-time employment after graduation. However, difficulties appear if students cannot maintain a proper balance. Long shifts reduce study time and leave them too tired for classes. Some focus more on money than on learning, which harms academic results. Without discipline, they may feel overwhelmed by competing demands. The lack of energy can also affect health, and in extreme cases, students may lose their motivation for study. The negative effects can be reduced if students manage their time carefully. Making a clear budget helps avoid the need for excessive work hours. Choosing jobs with flexible schedules also allows them to attend classes regularly. Universities and families can offer guidance so that students learn to balance responsibilities. With better planning, part-time work becomes an opportunity instead of a burden.
Q8 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “Besides money, students also want practical experience that cannot be gained from lectures alone.”
A. Students often hope that part-time jobs will provide both income and professional exposure. | B. In addition to earning income, students seek real-world skills unavailable through lectures. | C. Besides covering expenses, many students value working alongside academic studies. | D. Practical experience is unnecessary for students, as money remains their only true concern.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
