566MP/NỀN TẢNG TỪ VỰNG - ĐỌC HIỂU/20 CHỦ ĐỀ TỪ VỰNG VÀ ĐỌC ĐIỀN - ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN/Chủ đề 15 Our Heritage (2).pdfok315 / 0 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:15missing:15
Passage passage-76a956ca86-01-da618b9eed (3 questions)
Many of the world’s most beautiful landscapes and historic monuments are now facing serious threats from modernization, mass tourism, and climate change. Without international cooperation, many treasures might be lost forever. To respond to this challenge, an organization has taken responsibility for uniting countries in their effort to safeguard heritage for all humanity. That organization is UNESCO, and its role has become vital in protecting both cultural and natural wonders. UNESCO does more than simply recognize sites; it also provides technical advice, funding, and education programs that help nations preserve their heritage. Emergency missions are sent when wars or disasters damage monuments, while training workshops teach local communities how to maintain traditions. By supporting cultural exchange and global awareness, UNESCO ensures that heritage continues to inspire future generations. Its efforts prove that protecting the past is essential for building a shared future.
Q1 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “Without international cooperation, many treasures might be lost forever.”
A. Valuable heritage sites will remain safe even when nations fail to cooperate. | B. If countries do not work together, valuable heritage sites could disappear permanently. | C. International cooperation guarantees that all cultural and natural sites are at risk. | D. Nations can easily protect their wonders without relying on support from others.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q2 The word “supporting” in paragraph 2 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______.
A. identifying | B. denying | C. promoting | D. discouraging
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q3 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. UNESCO only recognizes sites but does not provide any further support. | B. Protecting heritage is unnecessary for the future of global communities. | C. Local communities can protect monuments without training or international aid. | D. UNESCO helps preserve heritage through advice, funding, and education programs.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-76a956ca86-02-7a1cd7e7be (4 questions)
When people hear the word “heritage,” they may imagine ancient monuments or historic ruins. Yet many forms of heritage are “living,” because traditions are practiced every day and continue to change with society. Heritage, such as customs, rituals, and performances, is not locked in the past; it adapts to modern needs while still carrying memories of earlier generations. In Japan, the tea ceremony illustrates how living heritage adjusts to new contexts. Although the practice still emphasizes harmony and respect, it is now taught in schools, universities, and cultural centers for both locals and foreigners. In Vietnam, water puppetry once belonged only to village festivals but is now performed in modern theaters, supported by light and sound systems. These changes expand audiences while keeping the original spirit alive. Communities make these adaptations possible. Potters in Bat Trang design vases and bowls with patterns that suit contemporary houses, while musicians in many countries mix folk instruments with popular music. Even traditional festivals adopt digital promotion and tourism programs. Such examples reveal that traditions endure not by resisting change but by creatively engaging with the present.
Q4 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the examples of heritage?
A. rituals | B. customs | C. performances | D. memories
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q5 The phrase “the practice” in paragraph 2 refers to _______.
A. harmony | B. Japan | C. living heritage | D. the tea ceremony
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q6 The word “contemporary” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. modern | B. permanent | C. old-fashioned | D. collective
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q7 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Communities play no role in adapting heritage to suit contemporary life. | B. Water puppetry in Vietnam remains limited to small village festivals only. | C. Traditional customs survive only when they completely resist modern changes. | D. The Japanese tea ceremony is now taught in schools and cultural centers.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-76a956ca86-03-58ea068d1f (8 questions)
Heritage can be understood in two main forms: tangible and intangible. Tangible heritage refers to physical objects or places that people can see and touch. It includes monuments, temples, landscapes, and works of art that tell the story of human civilization. Intangible heritage, on the other hand, is made up of practices, skills, and knowledge that live in people’s memories and daily activities. Tangible heritage is often easier to identify and preserve because it exists in visible structures. The Great Wall of China, the Pyramids of Egypt, and Hội An Ancient Town in Vietnam are clear examples. These sites represent history through architecture, design, and construction techniques that have survived for centuries. Governments and organizations can restore damaged buildings, protect landscapes, and regulate tourism to keep them safe. Intangible heritage requires different approaches. It includes oral traditions, performing arts, social customs, and rituals that may disappear if not practiced regularly. Examples are Japanese Noh theater, the Indian festival of Diwali, or Vietnamese ca trù singing. Unlike monuments, these traditions rely on communities to continue teaching and performing them for younger generations. Both forms of heritage are equally important, yet they interact in special ways. A festival in a historic town combines tangible settings with intangible practices. A temple becomes meaningful only when rituals and beliefs are still alive. Understanding the difference helps people see how diverse heritage truly is and why protection must address both sides.
Q8 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the examples of tangible heritage?
A. temples | B. monuments | C. knowledge | D. landscapes
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

568MP/NỀN TẢNG TỪ VỰNG - ĐỌC HIỂU/20 CHỦ ĐỀ TỪ VỰNG VÀ ĐỌC ĐIỀN - ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN/Chủ đề 16 Jobs (2).pdfok315 / 0 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:15missing:15
Passage passage-e6e29d9c8f-01-69a9e8efea (3 questions)
Youth unemployment is a serious problem in many countries around the world. Every year, thousands of students graduate from high school or university but cannot find suitable jobs. One reason is that the number of young people looking for work is often higher than the number of available positions. Another reason is that some graduates do not have enough practical experience or the skills that employers require. As a result, many young people struggle financially and feel insecure about their future. To solve this problem, both governments and schools need to take action. Governments should create more job opportunities by supporting businesses and developing new industries. At the same time, schools and universities should focus on teaching practical skills, not only theory. Young people should also be more active in doing internships, volunteering, or part-time jobs to prepare themselves.
Q1 The word “require” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______.
A. demand | B. purchase | C. obtain | D. advertise
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. practical skills | B. part-time jobs | C. internships | D. young people
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q3 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Youth unemployment is caused only by the lack of available jobs. | B. All graduates have the necessary skills and experience that employers demand. | C. Governments should create more employment by supporting firms and industries. | D. Schools need to prepare themselves through internships or part-time jobs.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-e6e29d9c8f-02-5b9e465286 (4 questions)
After finishing university, some students decide not to begin their career immediately but to spend one year exploring new experiences. This break helps them relax and gives them time to think carefully about their career. During a gap year, students can do many meaningful activities. Some choose to travel abroad to discover different cultures and broaden their horizons. Others may join volunteer programs to help disadvantaged communities, which not only improves their social awareness but also develops teamwork and problem-solving skills. A gap year can also be a good time to take short courses or learn new skills that will be useful for their future jobs. Although a gap year requires careful planning and financial support, it can be very beneficial. Young people often return with more confidence, maturity, and motivation. They also have a clearer idea of what kind of career they want to pursue. In conclusion, taking a gap year before work is a valuable opportunity that helps students grow personally and professionally.
Q4 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “This break helps them relax and gives them time to think carefully about their career.”
A. Taking a gap year allows students to rest and reflect thoroughly on their future jobs. | B. A gap year allows students to avoid making any immediate decisions about their careers. | C. Students do not waste time during a gap year as they can relax and reconsider their studies. | D. Relaxation during a gap year makes students temporarily forget about their career plans.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q5 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 2 as one of the meaningful activities?
A. do internships | B. travel abroad | C. volunteer | D. learn new skills
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q6 The word “pursue” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. suit | B. chase | C. avoid | D. follow
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q7 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. A gap year prevents students from gaining teamwork or problem-solving skills. | B. A gap year can help students gain confidence, maturity, and clearer career goals. | C. Taking a gap year always guarantees students a successful career. | D. Students on a gap year usually waste time without doing meaningful activities.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-e6e29d9c8f-03-74e1efac9e (8 questions)
In the modern world, both hard skills and soft skills play a very important role in education and work. Today, employers not only look at academic degrees but also consider how candidates can work with others and adapt to challenges. This trend shows that knowledge alone is not enough, and they need a balance of technical ability and personal qualities. Hard skills are the technical abilities that can be measured or tested. They include things like computer programming, accounting, engineering, or foreign language proficiency. These skills are usually learned through formal education, training programs, or practice. Without hard skills, it is almost impossible for a person to perform the basic tasks required in their job. For example, a doctor must know medical procedures, and an architect must be able to design buildings. Soft skills, on the other hand, are more about personal qualities and communication. They include teamwork, leadership, creativity, time management, and problem-solving. Soft skills are harder to measure, but they are essential in the workplace. A person with strong soft skills can cooperate with colleagues, manage stress, and build good relationships. This makes them more successful and respected by others. Hard skills and soft skills cannot replace each other. Hard skills show what a person can do, while soft skills show how they do it. Only by combining both can people achieve long-term success in their careers and lives.
Q8 The word “they” in paragraph 1 refers to ______.
A. employers | B. others | C. candidates | D. challenges
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. Performing advanced tasks in a job depends heavily on having hard skills. | B. Lacking hard skills makes it very difficult for people to carry out their job duties. | C. Employers need hard skills in order to manage the basic requirements of their positions. | D. Employees cannot complete essential tasks at work if they do not have hard skills.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. creativity | B. teamwork | C. accounting | D. leadership
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

570MP/NỀN TẢNG TỪ VỰNG - ĐỌC HIỂU/20 CHỦ ĐỀ TỪ VỰNG VÀ ĐỌC ĐIỀN - ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN/Chủ đề 17 Travel and Entertainment (2).pdfok315 / 0 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:15missing:15
Passage passage-726de4867c-01-e8cba9f515 (3 questions)
Rather than relying solely on advertisements, people today are inspired by what they see in movies, music videos, and online platforms. A romantic film set in Paris, a K-pop concert in Seoul, or a travel vlog from Bali can easily turn these locations into must-visit destinations. The connection between emotion and imagery encourages travelers to follow in the footsteps of their favorite stars or stories. As scenes circulate on social media, interest spreads quickly, transforming once-unknown places into international attractions. Beyond inspiration, entertainment adds meaning to travel experiences. Local festivals, art shows, and street performances allow visitors to understand the spirit of a culture while enjoying themselves. These events provide jobs for artists, strengthen local pride, and create memories that last longer than photos. When entertainment and tourism work together, they form a powerful bridge between cultures, helping travelers see the world not just as spectators but as participants in a shared story.
Q1 The word “circulate” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______.
A. combine | B. pursue | C. renew | D. spread
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q2 The word “themselves” in paragraph 2 refers to _______.
A. art shows | B. visitors | C. local festivals | D. street performances
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q3 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Entertainment and tourism together can connect different cultures and people. | B. Advertisements are the only factor influencing people’s travel decisions. | C. Travelers today no longer care about cultural understanding or local traditions. | D. Festivals and art events have no impact on the local economy or communities.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-726de4867c-02-75ba6e0f8a (4 questions)
Cultural festivals offer visitors a chance to witness local traditions, music, dance, and art that reflect a nation’s identity. Events such as Holi in India, the Lantern Festival in Taiwan, or Oktoberfest in Germany create a joyful atmosphere that draws millions every year. These celebrations allow travelers to see a country’s culture in action rather than just reading about it in books or online. One reason festivals are so appealing is that they combine culture with entertainment. Tourists enjoy the colorful parades, traditional performances, and local food while learning about history and customs. Many travelers even plan their trips around festival seasons to join the celebrations. This kind of travel not only provides excitement but also helps preserve cultural heritage by keeping traditional arts and values alive. Moreover, cultural festivals bring strong economic benefits. When visitors attend, they spend on transportation, accommodation, and souvenirs, helping local businesses grow. At the same time, festivals promote understanding and friendship among people from different backgrounds. They remind us that travel is not just about sightseeing – it’s also about connecting, sharing joy, and appreciating the beauty of diversity.
Q4 The word “joyful” in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. meaningless | B. happy | C. avoidable | D. miserable
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q5 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? “One reason festivals are so appealing is that they combine culture with entertainment.”
A. Festivals are popular since they focus more on entertainment than on traditions. | B. Festivals are interesting mainly for their cultural meaning, not for enjoyment. | C. Festivals attract people because they offer both cultural experiences and fun activities. | D. Festivals appeal to visitors even though they do not include much cultural value.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q6 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 3 as one of the things visitors spend on?
A. transportation | B. sightseeing | C. accommodation | D. souvenirs
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q7 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Cultural festivals are mainly organized for local residents, not for travelers. | B. Cultural festivals help preserve traditions while providing enjoyable experiences. | C. Travelers attend festivals only to relax, not to learn about different cultures. | D. Today's festivals focus on more economic growth rather than cultural exchange.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-726de4867c-03-db009576fb (8 questions)
Hosting the Olympic Games has long been viewed as both a challenge and an opportunity for the host nation. Beyond the excitement of sports, the event serves as a global stage that showcases the country’s image, infrastructure, and hospitality to millions of international visitors. Cities like Tokyo, London, and Sydney have used the Games to promote themselves as modern, safe, and welcoming destinations, leaving lasting impressions on tourists worldwide. The most direct benefit of hosting the Olympics is the surge in tourism. During the event, hotels, restaurants, and entertainment venues are filled with visitors from every continent. Even after the Games end, many travelers continue to visit the city to see the iconic stadiums and parks featured on television. For example, Barcelona’s successful 1992 Olympics transformed it into one of Europe’s top tourist destinations. Another advantage lies in the improvement of infrastructure. To prepare for the Games, host cities often upgrade airports, public transport, and urban areas. These developments make the city more attractive and convenient for future visitors. Cultural programs and festivals organized alongside the Olympics also help introduce local traditions to a global audience, further strengthening tourism potential. However, the benefits depend on careful planning and sustainable management. Without clear strategies, the massive investments required can lead to debt or unused facilities. Still, when handled responsibly, hosting the Olympics becomes more than a sporting celebration. It then becomes a catalyst for tourism growth, cultural exchange, and international recognition that endures long after the closing ceremony.
Q8 The word “showcases” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by ______.
A. supplants | B. presents | C. hides | D. functions
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. Barcelona | B. Olympics | C. television | D. Europe
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. local traditions | B. airports | C. public transport | D. urban areas
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Passage passage-b5ea9793e2-01-dbb02c4704 (3 questions)
Education helps ASEAN students understand each other better and feel part of one community. Many universities in the region now welcome students from other member countries. Scholarships give more young people a chance to study abroad, even if their families cannot afford it. When students live and learn together, they begin to appreciate cultural differences and see how cooperation can bring benefits to all. These experiences make them more confident and open-minded. Scholarships and study programs also create a sense of shared purpose among ASEAN students. They realise that education is not only about personal success but also about helping their countries grow. When they return home, they use what they have learned to solve local problems and support community projects. Step by step, this exchange of ideas and values helps build a stronger, more connected ASEAN region.

Students who work hard can afford to pay for overseas education themselves. | D. Only a few privileged students receive funding for international university programs.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. Cultural exchange programs often make students less interested in cooperation. | B. Scholarships are only offered to students who can afford to study abroad. | C. ASEAN students mainly study overseas to enjoy different lifestyles. | D. Education encourages ASEAN students to connect and understand one another better.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-b5ea9793e2-02-0909a63267 (4 questions)
In today’s globalised and fast-changing world, no country can face every challenge alone. Problems such as climate change, health crises, poverty, and war have impacts that cross borders and affect millions of lives. International cooperation allows nations to share knowledge, technology, and experience, creating stronger and faster responses to global issues. By working together, countries build trust and develop collective solutions that bring long-term stability. Cooperation also promotes peace, dialogue, and cultural understanding. Through organisations like the United Nations, ASEAN, and WHO, countries can negotiate peacefully and prevent conflicts before they grow worse. Educational and cultural exchanges help citizens appreciate diversity and develop empathy for others. When people understand one another, they are more likely to support harmony instead of division. Beyond solving problems, working together brings opportunities for growth. Trade agreements, exchange programs, and research partnerships open new paths for development and innovation. By cooperating instead of competing, countries can create a fairer, safer, and more sustainable world for future generations.
Q4 According to paragraph 1, international cooperation allows countries to share all of the following, EXCEPT _______.
A. knowledge | B. poverty | C. technology | D. experience
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q5 The word “promotes” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. requires | B. decides | C. discourages | D. fosters
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q6 The word “development” in paragraph 3 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______.
A. evolution | B. degradation | C. prevention | D. consideration
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q7 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Countries can easily solve crises like wars and poverty without outside help. | B. International cooperation enables nations to handle global problems more effectively. | C. Global challenges are limited to specific regions and rarely affect other nations. | D. Cooperation often leads to more conflicts rather than peace and understanding.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-b5ea9793e2-03-de2f5ede4d (8 questions)
The Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) is a regional organisation established in 1989 to promote free trade, investment, and economic cooperation among countries in the Asia–Pacific region. Its main goal is to support sustainable growth, enhance regional integration, and strengthen connections between member economies. APEC also focuses on human resource development, digital transformation, and inclusive innovation to ensure that all members benefit from globalisation. Vietnam officially joined APEC in 1998, a time when the country was deepening its international integration after years of economic reform under the Đổi Mới policy. Joining APEC marked an important milestone, allowing Vietnam to expand trade relations, attract foreign investment, and participate in discussions shaping the region’s economic future. It was also a strategic step to enhance its international reputation and strengthen partnerships with developed economies. Through APEC, Vietnam has gained access to larger export markets, advanced technologies, and professional training opportunities. The organisation has helped the country improve its business environment, promote tourism, and enhance the competitiveness of small and medium-sized enterprises. Hosting APEC meetings, such as APEC 2017 in Da Nang, also increased Vietnam’s visibility and global influence. However, challenges remain, including unequal development levels and limited capacity to meet high standards of trade liberalisation. To overcome these, Vietnam continues to focus on innovation, digital skills, and policy reforms that align with APEC’s goals. Strengthening education and sustainable industries will help Vietnam make full use of APEC cooperation.
Q8 The word “established” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by ______.
A. recognised | B. demolished | C. set up | D. laid off
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Passage passage-7def503c0b-01-d745613c69 (3 questions)
Online shopping allows customers to browse and compare thousands of products from home or work. It offers convenience, quick payment methods, and home delivery services that save time and effort. Buyers can also read reviews and check detailed information before making a decision, which helps them feel more confident about their choices. However, they must rely on images and descriptions instead of seeing the products directly in person. In-store shopping, on the other hand, provides a more personal and hands-on experience. Customers can touch, try, and check the real quality of items before paying. They can also interact with sellers, ask questions, and receive advice immediately. The shopping atmosphere, background music, and face-to- face communication make the process more enjoyable and social. Although it takes more time, many people still prefer in-store shopping for the sense of trust it brings.
Q1 The word “they” in paragraph 1 refers to _______.
A. reviews | B. buyers | C. choices | D. home delivery services
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q2 Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? “Although it takes more time, many people still prefer in-store shopping for the sense of trust it brings.”
A. Many buyers like traditional stores, though they require a longer time to make payments. | B. Customers save time by avoiding stores and relying only on online product reviews. | C. Despite being slower, many shoppers choose direct shopping as it feels reliable. | D. People prefer offline shopping because they cannot trust digital platforms completely.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q3 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. In-store customers cannot touch or try the products before making payment. | B. Online shoppers often rely on pictures and descriptions instead of seeing real products. | C. Online shopping rarely includes reviews or detailed information for buyers. | D. Visiting physical stores saves both time and effort compared to online shopping.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-7def503c0b-02-b123f76c4a (4 questions)
Modern supermarkets have become an essential part of city life, offering customers a convenient and comfortable shopping experience. With wide aisles, clear organization, and air conditioning, shoppers can conveniently find everything they need in one place – from food and household goods to cosmetics and clothing. Most supermarkets display clear price tags, making it easy to compare costs and choose products that fit each customer’s budget. Another advantage is the variety of sales and discounts offered regularly. During weekends or special holidays, supermarkets launch big promotions that attract hundreds of buyers. Customers can save money while enjoying the excitement of finding great deals. Loyalty programs and membership cards also give frequent shoppers extra points or rewards, encouraging them to return. Finally, supermarkets provide a reliable refund and exchange policy, giving customers more confidence when buying products. If an item is broken or unsuitable, it can easily be returned or replaced. This professional service, combined with high product quality, makes modern supermarkets a trusted and enjoyable place for shopping.
Q4 The word “organization” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______.
A. requirement | B. company | C. appointment | D. arrangement
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q5 The word “frequent” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______.
A. rare | B. regular | C. current | D. reusable
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q6 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in the passage as one of the advantages of shopping in supermarkets?
A. free home delivery | B. clear prices and organised layout | C. discounts and membership rewards | D. easy refund and exchange policy
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q7 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Refund policies make customers feel less confident about their purchases. | B. Customers cannot exchange items at supermarkets once they have been paid for. | C. Shoppers never compare prices when buying in modern supermarkets. | D. Supermarkets attract buyers through discounts, promotions, and reward programs.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-7def503c0b-03-aa1ac39e28 (8 questions)
In today’s world, many people see money as the ultimate goal of life. They work hard, compete fiercely, and even measure success by how much they earn. However, money was never meant to define a person’s worth or happiness. To understand it correctly, we need to look back at its history and original purpose to see what it truly represents. In ancient times, people didn’t use money at all. They exchanged products directly—rice for salt, clothes for tools, or animals for food. This system, called barter, worked only when both sides needed what the other had. Later, people began using objects like shells, metal coins, or rare stones as a medium of exchange. These early forms of money made trading simpler, fairer, and more efficient. When real money appeared, it brought huge advantages. It allowed people to store value, measure wealth, and build more complex economies. Over time, money evolved into many forms, such as cash, credit cards, and now digital payments or online wallets. Unfortunately, as it became more powerful, people started treating it as the center of life rather than a helpful tool to improve living standards. As we can see, money itself is neither good nor bad – it’s just a means to achieve meaningful goals. Don’t see money as everything, but as something that helps create and exchange values. The right mindset is to make money serve you, not let it control who you are or what you value most.
Q8 The word “original” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by ______.
A. fake | B. honest | C. authentic | D. initial
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q9 The word “They” in paragraph 2 refers to ______.
A. people | B. ancient times | C. products | D. animals
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q10 Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 2 as one of the media of exchange in the past?
A. rare stones | B. shells | C. rice | D. metal coins
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q11 The word “wealth” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______.
A. motivation | B. abundance | C. richness | D. poverty
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. People should earn as much money as possible to generate value for society. | B. We should treat money as a tool for value creation and exchange, not as all needed for life. | C. Money becomes meaningful only when it is used for daily trading activities. | D. Lacking money proves that a person focuses more on moral rather than material values.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q13 Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage?
A. Early forms of money, such as shells and metal coins, made trading simpler and fairer. | B. People in ancient times used money mainly to show their social position and power. | C. The barter system allowed trade even when only one side needed the other’s goods. | D. Digital payments have replaced all other types of money in today’s world.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

606MP/TỪ VỰNG CHUYÊN SÂU/CHUYÊN SÂU ĐỌC HIỂU 8 CÂU/Rèn luyện và nâng cao kĩ năng xử lí bài Đọc hiểu 8 câu (Buổi 6).pdfok216 / 0 / 1616 / 16thpt_reading_passage:16missing:16
Passage passage-472d9d11e5-01-7f9a125d6f (8 questions)
Gender equality does not imply that women and men must become identical, but rather that their rights, responsibilities, and opportunities should not be determined by their gender at birth. Achieving gender equality requires that the interests, needs, and priorities of both women and men are considered equally and valued in all aspects of life. Equality between men and women is not only a fundamental human right but also a foundation for self-development and empowerment across both genders. In recent years, gender discrimination has decreased significantly worldwide, leading to notable progress. The number of female workers has been steadily increasing in many regions, which has broadened economic opportunities for women and contributed to economic growth. Moreover, there has been a rapid rise in female literacy rates, and gender gaps in education have been considerably reduced on a global scale. In the political sphere, more women are being elected to public office, strengthening women’s representation in government and policy-making. Despite these developments, gender equality in opportunities remains a challenging goal. Gender inequality continues not only locally but also around the world in various forms. The exact nature of gender gaps varies, but in the majority of countries, there are differences between men and women in decision-making power, economic participation, access to opportunities, and social expectations. (Adapted from C21 Smart)
Q1 The word determined in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to _______.
A. selected | B. decided | C. predicted | D. removed
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage
Q2 All of the following are mentioned as a feature that should be considered equally between men and women to achieve gender equality EXCEPT _______.
A. needs | B. skills | C. interests | D. priorities
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage
Q3 The word notable in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ________.
A. noticeable | B. satisfying | C. unimportant | D. necessary
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage

Passage passage-472d9d11e5-02-4462f9d82b (8 questions)
Gever Tulley is a computer scientist from California. In 2005, he started a summer programme for children called Tinkering School. Gever Tulley and his team helped the children to think big and create plans for innovative things they want to build. Children have made fantastic things since the school started, such as a rollercoaster, a rope bridge, tree houses, wooden motorbikes and boats. At Tinkering School, children get all kinds of materials like wood, metal, plastic and also real tools like knives, hammers, and power drills. The school has been around for many years now, but nobody has ever suffered a serious injury in all those years. This is because there are strict health and safety regulations they must follow. The children always learn how to use the tools safely and they must wear the right clothing and protection at all times. Gever Tulley’s ideas have worked very well. In 2011, he and a colleague decided to create a ‘real’ school, called Brightworks, in San Francisco. Brightworks is based on the same principles as Tinkering School. The students at Brightworks seem to love their school. We spoke to 16-year-old Tina Cooper. She has been a student at the school since last October. ‘Since I started here, I’ve never sat in a ‘normal’ class with a teacher,’ she told us. ‘But it’s been a very exciting experience. I’ve worked hard at my new school for eight months now, and there hasn’t been one single moment when I found it boring. Before, I was bored quite often.’ (Adapted from THiNK)

607MP/TỪ VỰNG CHUYÊN SÂU/CHUYÊN SÂU ĐỌC HIỂU 8 CÂU/Rèn luyện và nâng cao kĩ năng xử lí bài Đọc hiểu 8 câu (Buổi 7).pdfok216 / 0 / 1616 / 16thpt_reading_passage:16missing:16
Passage passage-dd8c3cbbd4-01-2b1b9fb206 (8 questions)
Recognised as a World Heritage Site in Viet Nam, Ha Long Bay attracts thousands of visitors every year, who come to enjoy its magnificent natural landscape. However, the bay is also facing many threats that may lead to serious damage to the site. Wildlife in Ha Long Bay is being destroyed by overfishing as this is the only job to support most of the inhabitants here. As a result, rare sea creatures may become extinct soon. Another problem is deforestation of mangrove forests. The mangroves, known as the plants native to Ha Long Bay, provide protection during storms, preserve the shoreline, and act as a natural filter that keeps the sea environment clean and healthy. Numerous wildlife species including plants and fish also depend on the mangroves. What is more, some markets in Ha Long Bay sell unusual relics and natural objects, such as coral pieces, sea shells, or unique stones, probably illegally removed from the protected areas. Many of these items, converted into rings, key chains, and other souvenirs, are purchased by unsuspecting tourists. Buying these items creates a black market leading to further destruction of Ha Long Bay. As eco-tourists, we can help to protect Ha Long Bay for future generations in several ways. For example, by sailing or fishing with the fishermen, we can share the benefits of tourism with the local community. This will help them to increase their income and reduce excessive fishing. We can also help local people to learn about the significance of the mangrove forests and help them to replant the mangroves. We should also avoid purchasing unusual souvenirs. As responsible tourists, we can discourage suspected traders or vendors by refusing to buy their illegal goods. (Adapted from English 11)

Passage passage-dd8c3cbbd4-02-608e5e855d (8 questions)
An important part of being an adult in the USA is becoming more independent from your parents. First, make sure you are living somewhere rather than at your parents' house. If your parents own the property in which you live, they will have a say in how you maintain that property. If you want to be independent and make your own decisions about how you live, you will need to obtain your own housing that is completely separate from your parents both physically and financially. Second, generate your own income and be as financially self-sufficient as possible. One of the main challenges in becoming independent as an adult is acquiring a sufficient income to allow you to live without the financial assistance of your parents. This can be particularly difficult if you are still a university student, but it is not impossible. Seek scholarships and part-time jobs. If you are no longer a student, find employment that offers a salary that can cover your expenses. It may be necessary to obtain multiple sources of income in order to become financially independent from your parents. Next, budget your expenses carefully. When first becoming financially independent, it may be necessary to cut back on some of your expenses and strictly stick to a budget. Determine exactly what you can afford in terms of rent, food, clothing, transportation, and entertainment based on your own income, and create a budget. While you may experience a dip in your standard of living at first, learning how to follow a budget and be self-sufficient will help you stay independent. Finally, avoid relying on your parents as a first resort option for help of any kind. This, in no way, means that you may never ask your parents for help again, it just means that as an independent adult, there should be other sources...

608MP/TỪ VỰNG CHUYÊN SÂU/CHUYÊN SÂU ĐỌC HIỂU 8 CÂU/Rèn luyện và nâng cao kĩ năng xử lí bài Đọc hiểu 8 câu (Buổi 8).pdfok216 / 0 / 1616 / 16thpt_reading_passage:16missing:16
Passage passage-60466e6aba-01-559a5e4fb7 (8 questions)
It is common for all of us to take on more tasks than our desired potential. This can often result in stress and tiredness. Learn the art of sharing work with your partners based on their skills and abilities. Before the start of the day, make a list of tasks that need your immediate attention as unimportant tasks can take up much of your precious time. Some tasks need to be completed on that day only while other unimportant tasks could be carried forward to the next day. In short, prioritise your tasks to focus on those that are more important. Carry a planner or notebook with you and list all the tasks that come to your mind. Make a simple "To Do" list before the start of the day, prioritise the tasks, and make sure that they are attainable. To better manage your time-management skills, you may think of making 3 lists: work, home, and personal. Stress often occurs when we accept more work than our ability. The result is that our body starts feeling tired, which can affect our productivity. Instead, share tasks with your partners and make sure to leave some time for relaxation. Most of the successful men and women have one thing in common. They start their day early as it gives them time to sit, think, and plan their day. When you get up early, you are more calm, creative, and clear-headed. As the day progresses, your energy levels start going down, which affects your productivity and your performance as well. (Adapted from English 11)

Passage passage-60466e6aba-02-3aef17e50f (8 questions)
In February 2010, Dr. Choi Young Suk decided to retire early at the age of 52 when she was about to be appointed as vice principal of Daegu University. Dr. Choi's husband, Kwon Jang Soo, also gave up his car dealing business in South Korea to accompany her to Da Lat. In July 2013, with an investment gained from the sponsorship of the Korea International Cooperation Agency (KOICA) and her own relations, Dr. Choi founded a Special Need Education Assistance Centre at Hoa Phong Lan Handicapped School in Da Lat. The Korean educator also incorporates games and activities into her classes to boost their reactions to language and music. Students are instructed to make dishes and record their own activities in videos and photos. The video clips and photos will later be shown to the students to spur on their responses to language and spontaneous attempts to speak. The woman showed them how to do mini-embroidery paintings on coffee companies' product labels or girls' ornaments. She then marketed and sold the students' handicraft items to her Korean friends and companies. "Selling the products for money isn't as important as helping the kids understand that despite their physical disabilities, they still can do useful things, which is the purpose of special need education," Dr. Choi stressed. She usually spends her weekends travelling to remote areas in different provinces to donate bicycles and necessities and help build small houses for needy locals. "My husband and I still have some dozens of years more to devote to people, particularly kids in Viet Nam. I wish to be buried here after my death," she said. (Adapted from English 11)
Q9 As stated in paragraph 1, Dr. Choi Young Suk _________.
A. had lived in Da Lat before 2010 | B. took early retirement at 52 | C. was promoted to a senior position | D. gave up her car dealing business
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage

Passage passage-771935edc2-01-dd1dbdcb92 (8 questions)
As you scroll through your social media feed or browse a news website, what is it that prompts you to read on? The chances are that it’s the images that attract your attention. Online content that is accompanied by an image is more likely to prompt engagement, in the form of clicks, likes and shares, than straightforward text. This phenomenon is especially important to digital marketers. They will tell you that on average people only read around 20 percent of the text on a web page. When it comes to remembering information, it is said that we retain around 80 percent of what we see versus only 20 percent of what we read and just 10 percent of what we hear. Although these figures are criticised by experts as an oversimplification, visuals do play an important role in how we deal with content in an information-rich world. Producing eye-catching visuals to attach to digital content is vital for anyone who wants to get their message across to the maximum number of people. It’s not only in the commercial world that the power of the image is being recognised. Governments and NGOs such as the World Health Organisation are acknowledging the importance of social media in communicating public health messages. Research into social media engagement during one recent disease outbreak, for example, found that it was image-based platforms such as Instagram that were most effective in communicating vital health messages. Whilst there is some concern amongst healthcare professionals that what starts off as helpful messages can easily get distorted as they are shared and reshared, specialists say that social media has huge potential for promoting public health. Political journalists and campaigners have also long understood the power of an image. In a social media age, though, just posti...

Passage passage-771935edc2-02-6d1efe35ec (8 questions)
information about digital marketing on Facebook, AI can redo the task, but this time focus only on marketing with Facebook. However, the use of AI has some certain drawbacks. One of the key concerns is the potential bias in the responses given by AI. In 2022, researchers at Stanford University found that AI generated toxic and biassed language when responding to certain topics and phrases. Another potential risk of AI is that it could be used to create fake news, videos or other forms of media. This could be used to spread false information. In brief, AI is likely to transform the way we interact with technology and save us time and effort in studies and work due to its efficiency, but it can also result in potential risks such as discriminatory language or the creation of fake news. (Adapted from English Discovery)

Passage passage-7ef317a388-01-430f16ad80 (8 questions)
Each year, several organisations publish a list of world cities with the best quality of life. The organisations look at various factors to make their lists: a stable government, little crime, and good public transportation. Cities with a good quality of life are also usually close to nature, have attractive buildings, access to museums, nice weather, and a clean environment. For residents of these cities, life is generally great. However, even the best cities have some issues. In addition, approximately half of the world's population lives in cities with a lower quality of life. These people may dream of migrating somewhere better, but this may not be possible. A more feasible alternative is for these people to find a way to improve the quality of life in their own communities. Erik Ahlström provides a good example of this kind of personal action. Several years ago, he moved to Stockholm, the capital of Sweden. In general, Stockholm has an excellent quality of life. However, Ahlström felt that there was too much trash on the city streets. He decided to combine picking up the trash with jogging. He called this new activity plogging, and soon other people were helping him. However, it has become so popular that people now go plogging in communities all over the world. Mary Clear and Pam Warhurst are also good examples. They live in Todmorden, a small town in northern England. They found some areas of land nobody was using and began growing vegetables, fruit, and herbs. Their idea was that local residents could pick and eat them. People from other places soon heard about this "incredible edible" idea and wanted to know more. Todmorden now has so many visitors that residents there joke that they have invented a new form of tourism called vegetable tourism. (Adapted from Ex...

A. attractive high-rise buildings | B. a stable government | C. low levels of crime | D. efficient public transport

Passage passage-7ef317a388-02-ed40d39563 (8 questions)
These days, there are many sports competitions that are a physical challenge to the athletes. Well- known examples include the soccer World Cup and the Olympic Games. There are other events, however, that are less well-known, but also very tough: the Mind Sports Olympiad, and the World Memory Championships. These events are not physical competitions, but mental ones. At the World Memory Championships, for example, competitors have to memorise people's names and faces, or random images. At an international memory competition in Indonesia in 2017, Yanjaa Wintersoul achieved two world records: one for remembering 212 names and faces in 15 minutes, and one for remembering 354 images in five minutes. The people who win gold at these events are very good at using their minds and their memory, but anybody can improve their memory. One method has been known for a long time: the "memory journey". A famous Roman named Cicero wrote about it more than 2,000 years ago. The process is simple. First, think about a familiar place or journey, such as the rooms in your home. Next, think of some items to remember and connect each item in your mind to one room, or one location on the journey. Funny, crazy, or interesting connections are best because they are easier to remember. Finally, "walk" the journey in your mind and "see" the items in each location. Imagine you need to remember a shopping list. You might connect each item to the rooms in your home. For example, you might imagine seeing some bananas "sleeping" in your bed, watching some eggs rolling down your stairs, or swimming across a river of orange juice outside your front door... Then, when you go shopping, you "walk" from your bedroom to your front door in your mind and "see" what you need to buy. (Adapted from Explore New Worl...

620MP/TỪ VỰNG CHUYÊN SÂU/CHUYÊN SÂU ĐỌC HIỂU 10 CÂU/Rèn luyện và nâng cao kĩ năng xử lí bài Đọc hiểu 10 câu (Buổi 7).pdfok220 / 0 / 2020 / 20thpt_reading_passage:20missing:20
Passage passage-046ed17a8f-01-1bb5ce932c (10 questions)
Zoos are hugely popular attractions for adults and children alike. But are they actually a good thing? Critics of zoos would argue that animals often suffer physically and mentally by being enclosed. Even the best artificial environments can't come close to matching the space, diversity, and freedom that animals have in their natural habitats. This deprivation causes many zoo animals to become stressed or mentally ill. Capturing animals in the wild also causes much suffering by splitting up families. Some zoos make animals behave unnaturally: for example, marine parks often force dolphins and whales to perform tricks. These mammals may die decades earlier than their wild relatives, and some even try to commit suicide. On the other hand, by bringing people and animals together, zoos have the potential to educate the public about conservation issues and inspire people to protect animals and their habitats. Some zoos provide a safe environment for animals which have been mistreated in circuses, or pets which have been abandoned. Zoos also carry out important research into subjects like animal behaviour and how to treat illnesses. One of the most important modern functions of zoos is supporting international breeding programmes, particularly for endangered species. [I] In the wild, some of the rarest species have difficulty in finding mates and breeding, and they might also be threatened by poachers, loss of their habitat and predators. [II] In addition, as numbers of some wild species drop, there is an increased danger of populations becoming too genetically similar. [III] Breeding programmes provide a safeguard: zoo-bred animals can be released into the wild to increase genetic diversity. [IV] However, opponents of zoos say that the vast majority of captive breeding progr...

A. Animals will be forced to get used to artificial environments. | B. Dolphins and whales always die earlier than other wild animals. | C. Caged animals may suffer from mental health problems. | D. Some animals start to exhibit strange behaviours.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage

Passage passage-046ed17a8f-02-d80b34741e (10 questions)
In recent years, climate change has been a much talked about issue, with many debates over its possible impact on the world, and some even going so far as to question its very existence. However, with the undeniable increase in greenhouse emissions on Earth, there can be no doubt that the Earth is becoming warmer, and scientific studies have proven that if we carry on living the way we do, then global temperatures will rise even more. So, what exactly does this mean for the future of our planet? In chilly places like the UK, we may think to ourselves: ‘Finally, warmer weather!’ [I] But global warming will in fact cause extreme weather conditions, meaning that our winters would be even colder than they are now. [II] More than that, warmer temperatures will lead to the melting of ice in the north and south poles, which in turn will lead to rising sea levels across the globe. [III] This is particularly troubling for low coastal regions such as Florida in the USA which would be among the first to be flooded, along with many islands around the world, including the United Kingdom. [IV] So where will we be able to live? Unless we can find a way to evolve into mermaids and mermen, which doesn’t look promising, the only solution is to move elsewhere. There will be little choice left once a large proportion of the land is lost to the sea, and other remaining places will be so hot that they will be absolutely impossible to survive in. Countries that we consider to be hot these days will be even more so if temperatures increase by 5, 10, even 15 degrees; countries like Australia and India, among others. It is possible, then, that the once-frozen poles of the Arctic and Antarctica will be suitable enough to live in, having melted their ice and warmed up. It’s a scary thought to imag...

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage

621MP/TỪ VỰNG CHUYÊN SÂU/CHUYÊN SÂU ĐỌC HIỂU 10 CÂU/Rèn luyện và nâng cao kĩ năng xử lí bài Đọc hiểu 10 câu (Buổi 8).pdfok220 / 0 / 2020 / 20thpt_reading_passage:20missing:20
Passage passage-a1045ccae9-01-f61e23a678 (10 questions)
[I] Songdo International Business District in South Korea was started from scratch in 2005 and is still being built for the future. [II] Its construction is based on the world's best technologies and eco- friendly practices. [III] Located about 60 km southwest of Seoul, this 53.3 square kilometre eco-city has been planned around a central park and designed so that every resident can walk to work in the business district. [IV] The waste disposal system here is also innovative. There are no rubbish trucks or waste disposal bins in the streets. Instead, all household and office waste is sent directly through an underground network of tunnels to waste processing centres. The waste is then automatically sorted, deodorised and treated. The aim is to eventually convert it into renewable energy for the community. Sensors are widely used to monitor temperature, energy use and traffic flow. These sensors can warn the local authority about any problems and allow inhabitants to control the heating systems or household devices. High-speed Wi-Fi is provided everywhere. Government offices and schools are connected to the network. This allows office workers to use teleconferencing for meetings and students to attend classes at home. Attracting inhabitants, however, has been slow. Five years after its launch, only about 20% of the commercial space was occupied. But the developers are very optimistic about this city of the future. By 2020, they predict that more than 65,000 people will live and work in Songdo. They believe that it is on the right track to become one of the smartest and greenest cities in the world. (Adapted from English 11)
Q1 According to paragraph 1, Songdo has been built to allow its citizens to ________.
A. make use of public transportation | B. walk to work in the business district | C. travel to different parts of the country | D. travel to Seoul more easily
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage

Passage passage-a1045ccae9-02-1268dd5b5d (10 questions)
The latest World Happiness Report says that prosperity is not the main reason for happiness. If you suffer real hardship, you are unlikely to be happy, but once your basic needs are met, money and material things become less of a necessity. Happiness depends more on recognising the things you have and appreciating them, rather than getting more things. Yes, money can buy you the latest smartphone, tablet or fashion item, but this enjoyment is usually short-lived. It seems that deep, long-lasting happiness comes from intangible things rather than things like smartphones. One essential factor is human relationships. People who have the support of family members and also have strong friendships are more likely to be happy. [I] Feeling protected and respected and knowing you can trust in the people around you is vital. [II] Performing acts of kindness and generosity on a regular basis, for example, listening to a friend in need or carrying a neighbour's shopping, will make you feel on top of the world. [III] Even a simple smile can work wonders. [IV] It's not surprising that health is another key contributor to happiness. Poor health will certainly make you feel down in the mouth. But being healthy and staying healthy requires some effort. A healthy diet is crucial and so is regular exercise. Laziness will not make you happy. Exercising for twenty to thirty minutes a day helps to reduce stress and anxiety and makes you feel more positive and optimistic because it releases endorphins. So, if you've been feeling blue and worrying too much about your exams, get exercising. You'll also find that you sleep better. Talking of sleep, do you often wake up feeling miserable? If so, it's probably because you haven't had enough of it. Teenagers tend to go to bed too late and have to g...

A. guaranteed | B. valued | C. distributed | D. satisfied
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage

Many teenagers lack sleep, which is mainly caused by waking up too early in the morning rather than | C. Teenagers often stay up too late and wake up early, leading to insufficient sleep for many of them
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage

Happiness mainly comes from strong social connections, performing acts of kindness, maintaining
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage
622MP/TỪ VỰNG CHUYÊN SÂU/CHUYÊN SÂU ĐỌC HIỂU 10 CÂU/Rèn luyện và nâng cao kĩ năng xử lí bài Đọc hiểu 10 câu (Buổi 9).pdfok220 / 0 / 2020 / 20thpt_reading_passage:20missing:20
Passage passage-24b18e7b14-01-18ecd5445c (10 questions)
The Centre for Energy and Green Growth Research (CEGR), established in 1998, is a non-profit research and consulting organisation that aims to promote sustainable development in Vietnam. As a non- governmental member of the Vietnam Union of Science and Technology Associations (VUSTA), CEGR has made significant contributions to the development of sustainable energy and green growth in Vietnam. The first goal of CEGR is to promote renewable energy sources. [I] CEGR has been working closely with the government, private sectors and civil societies to promote policies and initiatives that encourage the growth of renewable energy in Vietnam. [II] In 2019, the country added 376 MW of wind power capacity, with over 80% of the capacity located in the central and southern regions of the country. [III] CEGR has actively developed policies and strategies to promote wind power. [IV] This includes the development of a wind power roadmap for Vietnam, which outlines key targets for the sector's growth. In addition, CEGR has been actively promoting sustainable development in Vietnam. It has developed sustainable transport strategies for the country in order to reduce greenhouse emissions. The strategies involve promoting low-carbon transport options such as electric vehicles and public transport systems. Furthermore, CEGR has assisted Vietnamese farmers in developing sustainable agricultural practices to reduce emissions and improve energy efficiency. Overall, CEGR's contributions in Vietnam have been significant, and the organisation's work has had a major positive impact on the country's energy sector. Its efforts have aided in the increased use of renewable energy sources and the promotion of sustainable development practices, both of which will have long-term benefits for the enviro...
Q1 What is NOT indicated about CEGR in paragraph 1?
A. It focuses on promoting sustainable development in Vietnam. | B. It operates as a non-profit research and consulting organisation. | C. It is affiliated with the Vietnam Union of Science and Technology Associations (VUSTA). | D. It primarily focuses on providing financial support for green projects in Vietnam.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage

A. [I] | B. [II] | C. [III] | D. [IV]

It has implemented transport systems to eliminate all greenhouse gas emissions nationwide. | C. It has developed strategies to reduce transport use to bring about sustainability for the country. | D. Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage
Passage passage-24b18e7b14-02-b60c7700aa (10 questions)
information about the "new" land to England. [III] In 1787, the British government started sending criminals and poor people to Australia. [IV] Later, free settlers came to Australia to start farms. In 1850, gold was discovered there, so more and more people came from Europe and China hoping to get rich. Most of them never returned home, so the population began to grow. Australia has a huge amount of land. In the 1940s, the government decided that the best way to develop the country was to invite more settlers to come from Europe. To attract immigrants, they offered money and other forms of help. More than one million people from Britain moved to Australia, along with several hundred thousand Europeans who had lost their homes in the Second World War. Migration is still growing. In 2017, more than 262,000 people went to Australia to stay permanently. Four main types of people settle there: Some go there because employment and business opportunities are better than in their home country. Others are the children, parents, or other relatives of immigrants who have already become Australian citizens themselves. A third group is refugees who are escaping war or political problems in their home countries. Finally, there is a small number of Australians who previously migrated to another country and now want to come home. At the same time, Australia also sends immigrants to other countries - a smaller number. Each year, about 60,000 Australians go to live permanently in other countries. With people from so many cultures migrating in and out, Australia is truly a diverse nation. (Adapted from Explore New Worlds)
Q11 What is indicated about Australia in paragraph 1?
A. It is the most culturally diverse nation. | B. More than half of its population is from other countries. | C. It is regarded as a melting pot. | D. At least 40% of its population settles in foreign countries.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2R.02, thpt_reading_passage

Passage passage-620c928818-01-789e11c27f (1 questions)
ĐỌC ĐIỀN KHUYẾT THÔNG TIN (BUỔI 8) Plus 2: Luyện đọc điền và đọc hiểu chuyên sâu Mark the letter A, B, C, or D on your answer sheet to indicate the correct option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 1 to 6. Helping those in need is a fundamental aspect of building a compassionate society. (1) _______, we foster a sense of unity and empathy within our communities. Moreover, assisting those less fortunate not only fulfils a moral duty but also (2) ______ between individuals. Because everyone deserves to live with dignity, it is imperative that we offer support to those facing hardships. By lending a helping hand, we can make a person feel valued and respected, (3) ______. In our daily lives, simple acts of kindness can have (4) _____. Whether it's offering a listening ear, donating food to a local shelter, (5) ______, each action contributes to a brighter future for all. Furthermore, by engaging in acts of service, we cultivate a culture of compassion that enriches (6) ______. Helping those in need is not just a responsibility; it is a privilege that brings us closer together as human beings.
Q19 C. the burning of fossil fuels D. if the burning of fossil fuels
C. the burning of fossil fuels | D. if the burning of fossil fuels
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Passage passage-620c928818-01-789e11c27f (1 questions)
ĐỌC ĐIỀN KHUYẾT THÔNG TIN (BUỔI 8) Plus 2: Luyện đọc điền và đọc hiểu chuyên sâu Mark the letter A, B, C, or D on your answer sheet to indicate the correct option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 1 to 6. Helping those in need is a fundamental aspect of building a compassionate society. (1) _______, we foster a sense of unity and empathy within our communities. Moreover, assisting those less fortunate not only fulfils a moral duty but also (2) ______ between individuals. Because everyone deserves to live with dignity, it is imperative that we offer support to those facing hardships. By lending a helping hand, we can make a person feel valued and respected, (3) ______. In our daily lives, simple acts of kindness can have (4) _____. Whether it's offering a listening ear, donating food to a local shelter, (5) ______, each action contributes to a brighter future for all. Furthermore, by engaging in acts of service, we cultivate a culture of compassion that enriches (6) ______. Helping those in need is not just a responsibility; it is a privilege that brings us closer together as human beings.
Q19 C. the burning of fossil fuels D. if the burning of fossil fuels
C. the burning of fossil fuels | D. if the burning of fossil fuels

660MP/TỪ VỰNG CHUYÊN SÂU/ĐỌC HIỂU CHUYÊN SÂU THEO CHỦ ĐỀ/01. Chuyên sâu Đọc điền - Đọc hiểu chủ đề The generation gap.pdfok210 / 5 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:10missing:10
Passage passage-2dc30ac975-02-4b64dbf7f6 (5 questions)
As time goes by, customs and traditions, which help shape the identity of an individual, change. That is why people belonging to different generations don't usually share the same values and beliefs. The difference in politics, culture, and other areas of life is what separates the generations and is often described as a generation gap. This can lead to frequent disagreements or even conflicts between the generations in a family. Therefore, bridging the generation gap is important. Below are some tips on how different generations in a family can connect. First, all generations should be open to new ideas. Having more life experiences, parents often think that they know what is best for their children. However, the world is changing all the time. So, parents should keep an open mind about new ideas or things that probably didn't even exist when they were growing up. For their part, children should also be willing to listen to their parents and try to learn important life lessons from them. Second, it is important for parents and children to make time for each other every day. Poor communication or lack of it can harm the relationship between family members. They should make an effort to listen to each other. For example, start by asking your parents about how they spend their day at work. Finding some enjoyable activities that you can all do together is the best way to strengthen family bonds. In conclusion, healthy family love can help bridge the generation gap. Parents and children love each other for who they are, no matter what they say, think, or do. Even if people might not approve of what other family members do or say, they are still family and always will be. (Adapted from Global Success Workbook) cho người khác!
Q6 Which best serves as the title for the passage?
A. The Role of Grandparents | B. Bridging the Generation Gap | C. A Healthy and Loving Family | D. The Generation Gap in Extended Families
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. political differences | B. family connections | C. values and beliefs | D. customs and traditions
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. features | B. appointments | C. solutions | D. arguments
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Q9 The word their in paragraph 2 refers to _______.
A. children | B. parents | C. life lessons | D. new ideas and things
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. Having a wealth of life experiences ensures that parents can support their children best. | B. An enjoyable activity that all family members can get involved in creates strong bonds. | C. Family relationships can get worse as a result of poor communication or lack of it. | D. Healthy family love means love, care, loyalty, and respect for family members.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-2dc30ac975-03-89360c05fe (5 questions)
Adolescence is a difficult time for both parents and teenagers as it is a period of physical, social and emotional change. Physically, as a dependent pre-teen child, you are being transformed into an independent young adult. In general, emotionally and socially, adolescents like you are experiencing profound changes in terms of freedom, privacy, interests, decisions and opinions. First, you want more freedom to choose who to see and when. You used to be most happy in the company of your parents. Now you want to spend some more time on your own. Second, your parents made most decisions before adolescence. But now you want to decide things for yourselves. You hate being told what to do all the time. Above all, adolescents have strong opinions and are idealistic. You see the world differently, develop your own views and your own sense of right or wrong. You feel you have all the answers while adults don't. All these perfectly normal changes will affect teenagers' relationship with their parents. So what can you, as adolescents, do to stay on good terms with your parents? Firstly, let your parents know that you still love and value them. Secondly, try to agree to rules and boundaries and prove to be dependable. Keep your words and stick to rules. Thirdly, try to understand why they might be irritated by your behaviour. Show your parents that you are capable of an independent life by taking care of your own school work and maintaining a healthy lifestyle. Finally, it's all about communication! Keep talking to your parents. The more disconnected they feel, the more critical, distrustful and controlling they tend to become. (Adapted from Friends Global)
Q11 Which best serves as the title for the passage?
A. Navigating Adolescence: Maintaining a Positive Relationship with Parents | B. Expert Advice: Don’t Give Too Much Freedom to Your Teenage Kids | C. A Strong Desire for Complete Privacy and Independence during Teenage Years | D. Parent-Teenage Relationship Getting Worse Due to the Generation Gap
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. rather clear | B. very great | C. very strange | D. quite late
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. wanted to spend some time alone | B. held different viewpoints from their parents | C. were the happiest when they were with their parents | D. developed their own sense of right or wrong
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. changes | B. adolescents | C. parents | D. relationships
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Parents can become controlling and distrustful when their kids turn into teenagers. | D. To show their parents that they are reliable, teens should go to school by themselves .
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

661MP/TỪ VỰNG CHUYÊN SÂU/ĐỌC HIỂU CHUYÊN SÂU THEO CHỦ ĐỀ/02. Chuyên sâu Đọc điền - Đọc hiểu chủ đề Being independent.pdfok210 / 5 / 1515 / 15hsa_cloze_text:10missing:10
Passage passage-4ace31f12d-02-d0d4a81fa4 (5 questions)
Most teenagers in Britain receive pocket money from their parents. They might have to do chores to get their pocket money, helping at home with tasks like cleaning, cooking, washing up, taking out the rubbish and ironing. A part-time job is an option for teenagers who don’t have pocket money or who want to earn extra money. Popular part-time jobs for teens include babysitting, delivering newspapers to houses, shop work and restaurant or café work. There are stringent government laws about children working. They can work a maximum of two hours a day on a school day but not during school hours. At weekends and during school holidays, they can work longer hours. In Britain, some children and teenagers have a bank account. There is no legal age limit at which you can open a bank account but a bank manager can decide whether to allow a child or young person to open an account. Parents can put pocket money directly into their child’s bank account. Some banks have a prepaid bank card designed for young people. Parents can add pocket money to the card then children can use it to pay for things, without carrying money. So, many teenagers are getting experience of working part-time, dealing with banks and deciding whether to save or spend their money. These are all steps towards becoming a financially independent adult and earning and looking after your own money. (Adapted from https://learnenglishteens.britishcouncil.org/) cho người khác!
Q6 Which best serves as the title for the passage?
A. Teens Manage Money: Steps Towards Financial Independence | B. Teens and Finance: How to Be More Careful with Money? | C. A Journey of Becoming Financially Independent | D. Different Jobs that Keep Teens Financially Independent
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. help with household chores | B. work part-time at a cafe’ | C. take care of babies or children | D. bring rubbish to landfills
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. strict | B. flexible | C. simple | D. clear
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Teenagers in Britain have to work hard to earn pocket money from their parents. | D. Some banks in Britain have a bank card specially designed for young people.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
Passage passage-4ace31f12d-03-ee2b93d32e (5 questions)
Research has shown that guiding students to become independent learners can result in improved academic outcomes, increased confidence and a greater ability to problem-solve, as opposed to relying on direction from others. Moreover, studies have also shown that independent learning is crucial for students’ transition to university, which is often an anxiety-inducing time for many young people. In a recent article posted by the Media Centre for Education Research Australia – an independent non-profit organisation based at Flinders University in South Australia – Professor Stella Vosniadou explains how, with the turn to online education during COVID-19, she identified self-regulated learning as a valuable tool that could help students profit from online instruction and ease this critical transition. “Learning can be improved, and it is more effective when students can control their motivational states, use effective strategies to manage their thinking, and reflect upon their learning processes and outcomes,” Professor Vosniadou said. “Self-regulated learners have the knowledge and skills required to reflect on their own learning, especially on the areas they need to improve”. According to Professor Vosnadiou, independent learning skills can help students benefit from online instruction, which has become common in many schools following the switch to remote and flexible learning. (Adapted from https://www.theeducatoronline.com)
Q11 What does the passage mainly discuss?
A. The benefits of distance learning amid the Covid-19 pandemic | B. Lifelong learning as an important feature of university students | C. How independent learning eases students’ transition into university | D. The impacts of self-regulated learning on students’ learning behaviours
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

A. develop | B. benefit | C. manage | D. appreciate
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text

Distance learning can negatively affect students’ ability to learn independently. | D. Adopting an independent learning style can contribute to better learning outcomes.
Ans: None (missing), ready=True, E2C.05, hsa_cloze_text
